					- Create Content for Own Platform
						- Blog Post Formula
						  #BlogPost
						  Note: this can be guest or ghost written
							- Choose one post topic
							  Single subject posts are better for SEO (Yoast) | #Syndication - https://workflowy.com/#/66255035dbb4
								- Choosing Content Idea
								  #IdeaGen-Content
									- Content Type
										- Money Magnets
										  #MoneyMagnets
											- Purpose is one or more of these:
												- Attract traffic
												- Earn trust (build influence)
												- Move people towards a sale
											- Money Magnet Types
											  Almost all of these leverage influencers to some degree
												- “How to” Post
												  #MoneyMagnets
													- Also see http://www.dangerandplay.com/2015/08/11/how-to-write-a-how-to-article/
													- or
														- Here is a problem or concern you have
														- Here is a solution I found
														- Here are the end results you can expect.
													- Money Magnet
														- Choose desired result/unsolved problem
														- Identify the benefit behind the benefit
															- Examples
																- Example 1: Benefit: Hit the ball farther from the tee on the golf course. Benefit behind the Benefit: You'll be the envy of all your buddies at the clubhouse. All your friends will scratch their heads in amazement when they see how far you drive from the tee.
																- Example 2: Benefit: Start a successful internet business. Benefit behind the Benefit: Escape the 9-to-5. Fire your boss. Live the lifestyle you deserve. No more sweating over whether or not you'll get laid off.
														- Create the How-to headline
														  #WritingHeadlines https://workflowy.com/#/e0d59686829d
														- Break it up with sections and subheads
															- Sections - Break the process up into at least three steps, but no more than five (1-2 paragraphs per step). Or many short specific steps
															- Subheads - should be a promise/benefit. At least one for the benefits, and another one for the how-to method
												- Tutorial Post
												  #MoneyMagnets
													- Overview
														- This tutorial post is similar to the “how to” post, but it demonstrates how to use a specific product or service.
														- This gives you an excellent opportunity to promote an affiliate product.
														- You just need to share your experience with the product/service. Share how you used the product or service to solve a problem. Walk your audience through the exact process you use.
														- The important thing here is to show how you used the product to reach a desired result and or overcome a roadblock that you know they resonate with.
													- Define the result (desired result/unsolved problem)
													  #Desire
													- Define why this solution is the best solution to reach that result
													- Create the How to headline
													  #WritingHeadlines
													- Outline the steps
														- Visualize yourself doing the process. Write down the steps.
														- If you have to, do a real-time run trough of the process anddocument it as you go
													- Identify the potential sticking points
														- Find places where people might get stuck because the instructionsare not clear or they might be afraid of taking the next step
														- Offer additional insight or encouragement in these spots
													- Show where the product or service fell short.
														- This makes your opinion more authentic and believable.
														- Weaknesses
														- How to overcome - It also gives you the chance to fill in the blanks for them. Show how you overcame the weakness in the product.
													- Edit
													- Have someone test the tutorial for you + fill any holes they find
													- Create a downloadable checklist
														- Your readers will love a checklist that they can download, print and reference.
													- Call to action
														- Invite them to buy the product or service. Include an affiliate link in the article. At the very least, include this at the end.
														- If it makes sense,include the link earlier in the post as well.
													- EXAMPLES
														- How to Buy a Men’s Jean Jacket – A Men’s Guide to Denim Jackets
															- Buying guides make for great tutorials. This one includes lots of images. It even has a video. At the end it gives links where to buy recommended jackets.
														- How to Create a Gorgeous Photography Web Site with SmugMug in 90 Minutes
															- This tutorial earns Ryan over 10k a month in affiliate commissions – from one tutorial!
															- He uses a lot of screencast videos. This could also be done with screenshot images.
												- Review Post
												  #MoneyMagnets
													- 1. Choose a product that is relevant to your audience
													- 2. Identify the problem that the product solves for your audience. Make that clear in the content.
													- 3. Tell your audience who the product is ideal for
													- 4. Express an opinion. Don’t hold back.
													- 5. Be honest
													- 6. Compare the product with other products that your audience might consider buying
													- 7. Use images or screenshots to show you have used it
													- 8. Feature both pros and cons
													- 9. Summarize what other reviews say (optional)
													- 10.Disclose that you make an affiliate commission if they buy through your link
													- 11. Include a call to action with an affiliate link
													- 12.Consider offering bonus content to anyone who buys the product through your link
													- **Advanced: **Free Giveaway Post
														- Anyways, let me break it down for you in steps:
															- Find a product that you'd like to promote as an affiliate, preferably one that you've used before, are familiar with and are confident in your recommendation to others (that's my rule for anything I promote).
															- Contact the creator or owner of that product, and try to get at least 1 free copy that you can give away to your audience. If you can't make that happen, buy some additional copies of your own to give away.
															- On your site (blogs work best, you'll see why), hold a contest to win those free copies of the product. To enter the contest, people have to leave a public comment about how that product would help them, and what they would do with it.  Make sure those comments are where other people can see them - this is why blogs work best, since they already have a visual commenting structure.
															- The links to that product that you tell people to view before they leave a comment should be your affiliate link for that product.
														- Why This Works:
															- This works because:
															- People love to get free stuff, so you'll get more people in front of that product (through your affiliate link), because of your contest.
															- When people leave their comments (there were almost 300 comments on that post), everyone else sees what everyone is saying about that product. That's promotional material - not from you but from other people, which is much more powerful. That's social proof at it's finest right there.
															- You're not directly promoting anything, and you're not seen as someone just trying to earn a buck. Everybody is a winner!
															- If the product is good, and has a good landing page (like Instant Article Factory did for me), then it will practically sell itself.
														- If you can contact the owner like I did, have him or her setup a special price just for you for a certain number of days, then you can increase your affiliate sales even more.
														- I never once told people they should buy Instant Article Factory, they made that decision on their own based on everyone else's recommendation and praise about the product.
												- “List of Names" Post
												  #MoneyMagnets
													- Decide Angle
														- praising/featuring/introducing some of the top up & coming and best bloggers in space
													- 1. Create a list of 3-5 potential titles for your “List of Names” roundup post
													- 2. Use Google, Alltop, Klout and Technorati to find online influencers to feature
													- 3. Using what you find in your research, narrow down and choose the titles that best fits the post you want to do.
													- 4. Look over their content. Write a paragraph for each influencer that explains why you have included them in your post.
													- 5. For each name in your list, find a blog post, article, video or course or book that you can recommend.
													- quotes + pic of them
													- 6. Check to see if any of them have affiliate links that you can use to link to them.
													- 7. Compile the list into a complete blog post including your paragraph and the link(s) for each name.
													- Ego-bait - email them I created a list of a bunch of people including x x x and you, just letting you know. They'll probably share with network
												- Interview Post
												  #MoneyMagnets
													- Interview posts are effective because they:
														- •Connect you with influencers and build your network
														- •Attract traffic from the following of the person that you interview
														- •Give you authority by association
													- SOP
														- Make a list of who you’d like to interview
															- Look at the most recent books published on your topic
															- List people that you’d like to collaborate or partner with
																- Danger & Play
															- Ask fans who they want you to interview
															- Post a requst to HelpAReporterOut
															- Share a never-before-seen interview
															- Additional Tips:
																- List some that are outside the usual choices
																- Avoid doing the same interviews that are currently making therounds in your market
																- Work your way up the chain. Start with “lower tier” interviews thatare easier to access. Then mention their names to getincreasingly bigger interviews.
																- Get introduced or referred by someone you know if possible.
																- Often your followers want to hear from other people that are "justlike them." You don’t have to always interview “big names.”
														- Decide what format you will use
															- At first do written interviews for your blog. Later you can try live audio interviews.
															- For written interviews
																- Email
																- Live - Slack.com or Skype
															- For live audio interviews use:
																- Skype + Pamela for Skype (PC) or Call Recorder (Mac)
														- Contact them for an interview
															- Look for contact information on their site or contact them through Twitter
															- **Copy and edit the template email**
															  NAME,
															  [show you're a fan] I enjoyed your recent post/book about INSERT TITLE OR TOPIC.
															  I particularly enjoyed the story/part/example about INSERT EXAMPLE.
															  [request + benefit] I’d love to feature an interview with you and promote your blog/book on my site, TITLE OF BRAND/BLOG.
															  [connect their expertise + your audience] My audience would love to hear your thoughts on RELEVANT TOPIC.
															  [social proof if relevant + recognisable] I’ve done similar interviews with NAME, NAME, NAME and they got a great response.
															  [CTA] If you’re interested, please hit reply and let me know.
															  [expectation management + appreciation] I’ll get right back to you with the questions. I know your time is valuable, so I’ll keep it brief.
															  Thank you,
															  YOUR NAME
															- When they agree, ask for a short bio, a headshot and the link they want you to promote in the interview
															- Set clear expectations of how you will use the interview
															- Let them know who your audience is so they can give answers that best fit your followers
															- Let them know when you need the interview answers back (or if you are doing a live audio interview you can set the date).
														- Prepare for the interview
															- Read their back story
															- Read some of their blog or book
															- Look them up on Wikipedia
														- Write Questions
															- sds
																- Decide what topic you will focus on. Your followers will enjoy the interview more if you go deeper on a specific topic.
																- Stick to 4-5 questions so that it doesn't get too long
																- Tell a story. Show the journey the interviewee went on.
															- Where they started
															- Where they wanted to get
															- What was the journey like for them
															- What did they do and how did they do it
															- What pitfalls or fears they faced
															- How did they overcome them
															- What were the results
															- How are they different now
															- What advice would they give someone now
															- What would they do differently
														- Send the questions
															- If it is a written interview, let them know when you need the questions back. Ask them if you can ask a round of follow up questions.
															- If you're going to do a live interview, send the questions at least a day in advance as a courtesy.
														- Scheduling tools
														  #Communication https://workflowy.com/#/04221b7da8c6
														- Set a date and time for the interview (LIVE ONLY)
														- Edit the interview
															- Stay true to what they said, but feel free to cut down to the most important stuff
														- Copy these into the bottom of the blog post
															- 1) Create interview headline
															  #WritingHeadlines https://workflowy.com/#/e0d59686829d
															- 2) Outline
															  #BlogPost-Outline https://workflowy.com/#/81561522ceb3
														- Transcription
														  #HabitLaboratory-Organisation https://workflowy.com/#/86c626739c27
														- Inform the interviewee
															- Let the interviewee know you published the interview
															- Ask if they can promote it to their followers
															- Invite the interviewee to participate in the comments
														- Thank the interviewee
															- Ego-bait - email them I created a list of a bunch of people including x x x and you, just letting you know. They'll probably share with network
													- EXAMPLES
														- How Two Women Used a Blog to Beat Their Kickstarter Campaign by 227%
															- This title uses specific results to get the reader hooked. It’s far more powerful to mention what they reader will learn rather than Interview with So-and-So in the title.
															- The post does a great job of telling the story and digging into some specifics for the reader.
														- David and Tom Kelley: How to Gain Creative Confidence at Work
															- Notice how this title combines the interview with a “how to” benefit. I like how this interview introduces the concept of creative confidence, explains why it is important and then asks for three pieces of advice.
														- Selling Rock from Outer Space: an Interview with ‘Meteor Man’ Geoff Notkin
															- This title uses curiosity really well. Selling rocks from outer space?
															- It indicates it is an interview and who it is with, but then explains who the person is (a meteor man) for anyone that is not familiar with his name.
															- The opening line is excellent and grabs the reader. What’s the oldest thing you’ve ever held in your hand?
													- Making sure to ask lots of intelligent questions e.g. www.thegoodlifeproject.com
													- research questions which will put their best content on podcast in interviews
													- Create a podcast with other experts
													- Organise a conference for niche
													- Interviewees
														- PODCAST: Reach out to academic researchers like social triggers
													- http://www.incomediary.com/how-to-land-a-list-interviewees
													- Interview real-time
														- Be direct and don't be embarassed to ask 'stupid' questions.
														- Don't ask multiple choice questions - what/why/how/why/who questions are good but would/should/do you think questions are bad.
														- Don't ask leading questions - ask directly or don't
														- Interrupt - to ask clarifying questions and stop rambling. Or follow up with a direct question. Or ask a variation question later
														- Repeat answers back for clarification or more detail (also can deliberately misparaphrase to catch lying)
														- Summarise info before and after sections
													- Multimedia Interviews
														- Create an outline for script, bring in guests to make creating content easier; don't waste people's time with 2 min intros; record unedited so then you can learn from them and not remove mistakes; title+description+podcast name+episode name eg timp - personal development etc ep name TIMP#11 or TIMP1should be kw-rich; have great podcast artwork; include calls to action within episode; make links pretty and easy to remember eg pretty link plugin or buy domain eg aaronfacebook.com; transcripts
														- Ask at end any closing remarks + what's got you excited
														- Media training - answering who I am in different lengths of time; typical questions about what I have experimented with, what's being a therapist like,
														- After interviewees discuss studies you should summarise what they just said whilst expressing amazement.
													- Related: Podcasts
													  #AudioMarketing https://workflowy.com/#/858df247a323
													- Learning Resources
														- http://mixergy.com/
														- risetothetop.com
												- Roundup Post
												  #MoneyMagnets
													- Types
														- Curation
															- Shoutout different influencers and their recent posts
															- Resource Round-up (best posts/books/sites available out there)
															- “follow friday” or “link love wednesday”
														- Mini Interview
															- Email them a question, say you're putting it on an up&coming blog + give them a relevant article
													- Ego-bait - email them I created a list of a bunch of people including x x x and you, just letting you know. They'll probably share with network
												- “Curated Expert Advice” Post
												  #MoneyMagnets
													- 1. Create a list of 3-5 potential topics and titles for your “Curated Expert Advice” roundup post
													- 2. Use Google, Alltop, Klout and Technorati to find online influencers to
													- 3. Using what you find in your research, narrow down and choose the titles that best fits the post you want to do.
													- 4. Create an outline for your post. This is a bulleted list of the sections or steps that you need to cover in your post.
													- For each section, find 2-4 links to resources that cover the topic. For each link/resources, write one or two sentences about what the reader will find. Use affiliate links where possible.
														- **Advanced:** Get a quote from them instead/also e.g. best advice on x topic, then post it next to other experts
															- eg http://thinktraffic.net/10-blogs-with-explosive-growth-to-learn-from http://thinktraffic.net/traffic-building-tips
															- eg 100 things I wish I knew before I started traveling
															- Ask the Experts - ask them to provide quote
													- 6. Compile the list into a complete blog post including headers for section, list of links and brief description for each link.
													- Ego-bait - email them I created a list of a bunch of people including x x x and you, just letting you know. They'll probably share with network
												- There is some SEO benefit, but the main goal is getting these people sharing with their audience, your write up thereby exposing your brand to them. + gains you Authority by Association
											- Related: _IBM Surveys_
											  #AudienceFeedback https://workflowy.com/#/767e11c178e0
										- Unassorted other styles of content
											- Other Types
												- Be Useful - List Post, Problem/SOlution, SAQ, Checklist, Ultimate Guide, Definition, Series, Stats, Frameworks (e.g. conceptual ideas like those of Steve Pavlina/Seth Godin/popular books etc)
												- Be Generous - Profile, Crowdsourced, Interview, Link Roundup, Quote, Best of the Web, Pick of the Week, People to Follow
												- Be Entertaining - Story, Satire, Cartoon, Meme, Parody
												- Be Timely - News, Trend, Issue
												- Be Engaging - Question, Answer, Challenge, Customer Showcase, Freebie, Contest
												- Be Controversial - What If, Debate, Attack, Prediction, Reaction
												- Be Promotional - Comparison, Project Showcase, Income Report, Company Update, Presentation, Best of, Product Update, Product Tips
												- Be Human - Inspirational/Holiday/Vulnerability/Behind the Scenes/Off-Topic/ Rant
												- Taboo
												- Unusual
												- Outrageous (suprise/anger/awe/fear)
												- Hilarious
												- Remarkable
												- Secrets
												- Content that evokes an emotional response is MUCH more likely to go viral
											- More Types
												- 400 words or less; 2000 words or more; Video (can be made into written/audio + video easily so very useful for my rants/explanations); ecourses; Workbooks; Interviews; Monthly reports;  reader-created ebooks; definitive guides; resource page; forums; books; events; store;; top 10 lists; routines; review of news/research; challenges; case studies of a person/website e.g. quicksprout/social triggers (if I was them..); free pdf in post (pay with a tweet)
												- LISTS: Shining Examples (to illustrate a point); Quick actions/ideas to implement; Round-up of influential people
												- Case studies/testimonials
												- You can also respond to a thought-provoking post in your topic, quoting liberally (with links and attribution, of course).
												- Flagship Content
												- focus on expertise (I have experience with dating habits especially. Paleo, HIT and gymnastics. Nootropics. Red pill) and real needs eg complacency, boredom, obesity (think how I went from newbie to expert, what lessons I learnt and objections I had)
												- How to change your life resource page (strategical way of addressing problems in your life eg how to choose goals, vision, anti vision, purpose. Then choosing career eg work backwards, my dream criteria means that I have to be location independent
												- essential information about how you understand their problem, options for solving the problem, examples of how you can help, and explanations of why you perfectly meet her needs
												- 1) Biggest tips
													- BeginnerGreen content - narrow beginner topics and never goes out of date, what newbies need to know
													- Intermediate - use chaos monkey theory to put it under pressure for anti fragility eg holidays, take on lots at same time, super habit trials
													- Advanced - you may have your routine but mix it up. Take on habit experiments (trials) for fun as routine can get boring. Add more variety
												- 4) FAQ about habits (may be better once I start getting questions from audience?)
													- Write for beginners/evergreen e.g. stop smoking, etc (or as a paid guide?)
												- 5) Message e.g. "you can do this, all it takes is time and effort" + back it up with how-to
												- 6) Research & Results - Ask audience an important question then compile the answers for another post
												- or a poll/survey e.g. which is the hardest habit to create? what area of life are you most struggling with?
												- 7) Jargon Buster glossary
												- 8) Product Database (reader reviews + mine)
												- 9) Case Studies e.g. students of mine that have learnt a lot
												- Series = landing page is kw-optimised, short posts keeps retention + absorption
												- + newsletter signup, ebook or exclusive membership content promoted at bottom
												- Manifesto is something like think traffic or Chris guillebeau long PDF manifestos. Initial post is more of a mission statement or vision for niche and blog
												- > save up 10 guest posts to use within 2 weeks rather than 2 a month (be everywhere)
											- 4 Learning Styles
												- WHY - they should be doing something
												- WHAT - behind the content (theory, history, information)
												- HOW -  step-by-step “do this then that” framework
												- WHAT IF - see real world examples and case studies.
											- Even more types
												- Conduct your own personal experiment. Document it by video and post the results on your blog. How it helps: You establish trust by showing people you are actually doing what you talk about.
												- Create a series of case studies of successful people in your niche. But don’t just interview them. Have them mentor you through the process step-by-step. How would they guide you if you were completely new to the topic? How it helps: You build relationships with other influencers and provide your readers with tons of value in the process.
												- What are the myths or misconceptions in your practice? Go out and test them, and tell your readers what you find out. How it helps: You build curiosity and gain readership by answering their often thought about questions.
												- Conduct a personal challenge that seems ridiculous or impossible. Update people regularly on your progress. How it helps: You attract followers by doing something epic and establish authority by positioning yourself as a leader.
												- Help a novice related to your field completely transform. Document their transformation and use it to inspire others. Create a contest to decide who is chosen for the best transformation. How it helps: You build social proof and get others interested in your offerings.
												- We can diverge and connect with people we wouldn’t normally connect with. If you teach Aikido, study Tai Chi for a while and tell people what you find out. If you’re a chef, go out and visit some organic farms and get a better appreciation for the earth food is grown in.
													- Sean Aiken shared his story about how he worked one job a week for an entire year to find his passion.
													- Katie West secretly coordinated outbursts of mass-laughter in public places.
													- Michael Roberts stayed active 20 hours a day by eating raw food for 30 days.
												- Arguing the opposite of what you believe with yourself. Eg Steve PAC dialogue why you should get a job
												- Writing a rant and then seeing what you’ve said.
												- Filler
													- short posts or inspiration (when lacking energy/creativity)
													- Ask the readers
													- Link to others
												- Guest Posts or Interviews (content created by others + good networking)
													- Ask the Experts: What's the Easiest Way to Become Healthier?
													- e.g mark sisson, etc etc all providing one tip each such as drink water, no carbs, etc
												- Case Studies - Case studies are great, because you're basically reporting on something. So, as far as the "hard-core thinking" goes, it's minimal, because your data becomes the content itself.
												- Monthly Reports - You know I'm a big fan of Monthly Reports. My monthly income reports have because the number one most viewed and most commented on post that I write, and honestly - it's one of the easiest posts that I write too. It's easy because again, a lot of it is just reporting and data, and secondly it's a lot of fun! Plus, since you're doing it monthly, it's something you KNOW you're going to write about, and something your readers have to look forward to as well.
												- Links to Other Blogs - You see this one a lot, and it's great because it's super easy, and super helpful for your audience. Get them connected to other people in your niche by linking to certain posts that you've read that you think would be great to pass on to your readers. In addition, the blogs you link to will mostly likely take notice, which may spark a profitable relationship in the future.
												- Links to Posts in Your Archive - Every day we get new people to visit our blog, so it's impossible for everyone to know everything you've ever written. That's why it's easy to get them to read killer posts that you've written in the past that might be buried in your archives, by simply listing a number of posts from the past that you'd like to share. Be sure to include a little snippet or summary, to get people excited about reading some of your older stuff.
												- Personal Stuff - Some of you may not be comfortable with sharing personal things on your blog, but if you don't mind, sharing personal things can be an easy filler post that actually does a lot of good. It helps show that you're a real person, and it gets your audience to connect with you on a more personal level. You're not just a blogger or a person who writes posts, you're A.
												- Talk in terms of my examples and language though, no point faking my personality, let it come out
												- Do something unheard of. e.g. transparent walkthrough of Niche Site Duel
												- Vulnerability/Authenticity e.g. drug usage, views on sex,> then can meet the guys/girls I want to because I’m honest and unashamed of who I am as a person, fuck some stupid societal pressure not to talk about these things. I’m a normal person, we all do this. real shit admitting learning game, spilling my secrets/embarassments series
												- Share personal stories, videos, failures/pain/struggles
												- advice, inspiration or entertainment
												- Ask the Experts: x question
													- e.g. http://thinktraffic.net/traffic-building-tips
													- Most important question that target audience wants answered
													- Email them after thanking them and include a habit.la link
												- List their gurus. This is one of the most socializable posts – you can target a tweet of the link to every guru mentioned. Hopefully they will RT and comment on the post. Here’s how it worked for one of Carol’s clients. Check out the comments on that post and you can see some of the gurus mentioned coming on to comment. Through this post, the client ended up being asked to one of his gurus asking him to be a regular guest poster for Small Business Trends, a great site for exposure to his prime leads.
												- Tell a customer success story of how they used the product or service.
												- Ask a question to learn readers’ biggest problems. Then, address these issues in future posts.
												- Make a prediction about where your industry is going. Everyone wants to know the future.
												- Have a contest and give away a valuable prize.
												- Do a list of fun/unusual/different ways to use their product.
												- Tell the company’s history and how the business overcame challenges to be successful today. Blogs are great for bonding with customers by sharing behind-the-scenes stuff that lets them get to know the business better.
												- Profile a longtime employee — or a brand-new one.
												- Link roundup. This can be an easy weekly feature – just use your newscatchers to round up interesting posts on a theme related to the client’s industry. Another great socializing opportunity, as you can notify each site they’re mentioned. Take a look at Kikolani’s madly successful Friday link feature, for example.
												- Prove an authority wrong
												- Add to a list post created by someone else
												- Look for comments on industry blogs and see what other people are saying
												- Check for holes in the competition
												- Check what’s trending in social media (youtube, stumbleupon, digg, etc.)
												- Write a post for a newbie – remember, new people are always arriving. Creating a 101-level series can allow the blog to create a good “start here” page.
												- Quotes from your niche – You’ll be amazed at how many people will retweet compendiums of quotes from famous people.
												- Videos from your niche
												- TED talks from your niche
												- Create a list of resources
												- Create a list of tools
												- Write a step-by-step tutorial
												- Publish an image roundup
												- Interview someone controversial
												- Make a list of people to follow on Twitter
												- Promote your competition
												- Predict the future of your niche
												- Detail a case study
												- Talk about how social media has transformed your niche
												- Tie your industry into pop culture
												- Respond to criticism
												- Publish a roundup of your best posts (great for SEO)
												- Create a Template
												- Make something hard easy
												- Make a list of essential industry books
									- Content Topic
										- Idea Generation
											- Go through Evernote
											- Idea Stream
												- www.feedly.com
												- Insights
												- Alerts
												- Google analytics
												- Google webmaster tools
												- Email reply
												- Survey via Twitter question / survey /
												- - Sort through Influencers KWs to find what people are looking for
												- On Amazon.com type in relevant kw's, look inside the cover (table of contents) to get ideas.
												- The point of this is we’re not starting with the keyword tool which is where most people start. They look at the keyword tool; they search for the classic higher search volume, good competition, rankable. Forget that. Let’s go to real people. Let’s be real marketers and solve problems. That’s where the money is. Start with this.
												- FUTURE
													- Coaching
													- Team
													- Forum
													- Video analytics
													- Heatmaps, split tests
													- Help desk
													- Local meetups
												- Insights are good.
													- You can have a look at Facebook insights and you can find out a lot about which posts of your Facebook page are most viral, which ones are being talked about more, which ones got the most views.
													- You can also see which things are coming through on alerts for your industry topic news.
													- You could even put your competitor’s terms for your alerts and go and see what they’re up to and create news around that type of activity. There’s Twilerts as well as Google alerts. So you can also pick up Twitter news.
												- Forums are fantastic.
													- I love forums for ideas. I’ve gone into forums before and posted a question. What’s your biggest challenge? I’ve created 8 video replies and posted all 8 videos and that turned into a traffic channel.
												- Then there’s Google Webmaster tools which is becoming increasingly important.
													- It shows you how many search queries you’re getting for certain phrases, the click through rate and the number of searches and your rank for that phrase. I’m increasingly focusing on this particular data. It’s very good, especially when you start off from scratch. You won’t have much data but you’ll quickly get your Webmaster tools firing and it’s going to point you in the right direction to create a continuous improvement with your content flow.
												- Email replies are critical.
													- On every single email you send out, I strongly recommend that you say, “Please reply.” “If you’ve got any questions hit reply.” This is counter intuitive; we think we’re going to be swamped. I send out over 70,000 emails a month and I get 1 or 2 replies a day. But the ones I get are fantastic. It’s really good feedback, it’s giving me ideas. One guy hit reply and said, “Do you guys do health checks for websites?” I turned that into an idea, we broadcast it, we made sales. In fact, interestingly, my web development team have doubled sales in the last 30 days from that one source.
												- Local meet ups are amazing.
													- Anything face to face is high value. I go along to a FastWebFormula meet up, I can sit there for 5 hours straight and listen to a lot of different questions, join in on amazing discussions and I’ll get plenty of ideas into my notepad that I’d like to talk about and share with other members.
												- Tools are great.
													- SEMRush, Ahrefs, etc. Google keyword research. I don’t use many tools anymore. They’re fine as a guide but if you do the things above this topic, you’ll get far better results because it’s real. Tools are more or less a guide, the navigation showing you between Frankfurt and Italy via Switzerland.
													- Yeah, great, you sort of get the hang of it. But when you get in the car and drive the route and listen to people and join in the conversations and observe things, which is the next point, that’s when you’re really going to find out stuff.
											- Look at the tables of content of bestselling books on your topic. Amazon's "Look Inside" feature is a great way to do this.
											- Look at popular posts on top blog in your market. Create your own version of those posts.
									- Content strategy/evaluation
									  #HabitLaboratory-ContentStrategy https://workflowy.com/#/85f749c60977
								- Choose one Keyword
								  #MarketResearch https://workflowy.com/#/839ad1c495d2
								- Evaluate idea:
									- Is this evergreen pillar content I can keep linking back to
									- Purpose is one or more of these:
										- Attract targeted traffic (ideal audience)
										- Earn trust (build influence)
										- Move people towards a sale
									- If money magnet type:
										- Money Magnet Formulas
										  #MoneyMagnets https://workflowy.com/#/b36ff259663c
								- Promise-based headline
								  #WritingHeadlines https://workflowy.com/#/e0d59686829d
								- Choose a call to action
								  #CallToAction
							- Outline
							  #BlogPost-Outline
								- Beginning
									- What am I going to tell them
									- Use teaser above 'Read more'
									- Benefits
									  #Desire https://workflowy.com/#/af1ec707ac0d
									- List interesting facts
										- e.g. specific kinds of fish you can find near location described, how much money in a market, time to learn a habit etc
										- Evidence
										  #Evidence https://workflowy.com/#/ac6c99e9bba5
									- Ask Questions
										- Conversational copy e.g. ask and answer straight away for value
										- Yes Questions (questions they subconsciously say yes to and agree with)
										- ((67375e54-f32b-4657-a4cf-705f7a422cee)) Question
								- Middle
									- Break it into 3-5 sections. You only need 100-200 words per section.
									- Most important points first
									- Tell them in a few different ways
									- Choose promise-based subheads H/2
									- Explain what it is
										- + #Evidence https://workflowy.com/#/ac6c99e9bba5
									- How to do it step-by-step +  give concrete examples
									- Steer clear of short and useless - cover a topic comprehensively, don't write for an arbitrary word count
								- End
									- Conclusion - summarise/Powerful statement
									- Call to Action
									  #CallToAction https://workflowy.com/#/a155ecb05a81
									- Unique Sign-off (sometimes?)
										- E.g. take care, well done, enjoy the decline.
										- Be the best you can be?
							- Write
							- Rewrites
							  #Rewrite https://workflowy.com/#/79fce44af8e3
							- Publish / Schedule the post
							  Write Daily, Publish Weekly
							- Aftermath
								- Use Buffer extension to create bit.ly link (public record of when page was first created)
								- Go to Buffer.com (to use long URL for brand recog) to Twitter 3x with 4 hour gap (4pm/8/12) e.g. Title, quote and benefit

									- Facebook post pics
									  And lastly, let’s quickly go over the size of the images and the process of uploading them to Facebook.
									  
									  I typically create an image that is 403×403 pixels square, knowing that the image will not be cropped or cut off when it appears in people’s feeds. I upload the image first, and then add in my status update and link to my content from there.
									  
									  According to Amy Porterfield, however, Facebook recently made a change giving preference to link-share posts. These types of posts actually show up more often in people’s feeds, and they use a different image size too.
									  
									  When you paste a link directly into your Facebook status area first, you’ll see some content automatically populate, including the title of your post, a description of it (which you can edit), and a spot for an image that is more rectangular in size. You can click through and select an image that’s already in your post, or you can upload your own. This is where you’d upload a unique image with some text perfectly suited for your Facebook fans, if you don’t have one in your post already.
									  
									  The size of this image should be 560 pixels wide by 292 pixels tall.
									- Tips
										- I could get so much more leverage out of sharing my posts and podcasts on Facebook if I took a little bit of extra time to create an image with some text and my logo on top of it, and then and only then I should share it on Facebook and other social media platforms.
										- Since that interview, I’ve been taking a little bit of extra time to create graphics just like Gary described, and the results have been amazing! If you’re on my Facebook page, you would have noticed a number of images that look similar to this:
										- Facebook Image for Post
										- In the noisy world of social media, graphics like this are more likely to be seen and shared, and since starting this strategy and adding it to my pipeline, Facebook has become the #1 referring website for Smart Passive Income.
											- If you have a VA or graphic designer on your team with the capability to create images like this, then of course you’d want to outsource this task to them.
											- But if you don’t have one, or prefer to do these yourself (which is what I do) it’ll take some time to learn how to do it quickly.
											- At first, it took me 15-20 minutes to complete an image, but now I’m at a point where it’s taking less than 5 – but even if it took 30 minutes, it would still be worth the effort.
											- If you’re not creating graphics like this, you’re doing yourself, your content, and your audience a total disservice.
											- The graphic and the text doesn’t have to be very fancy either. Here’s another post I just recently published with some very simple text, but the same kind of results:
											- Simpler Text on Image for Facebook
											- Below is a link to a blog post I wrote that will help you find images that you’re allowed to use for this type of situation:
											- The Ultimate Guide to Finding and Using Images on Your Blog
											- If you’re not into stock images or creative commons and don’t want to waste time finding images on your own, then you can take a screenshot of your own content or website, blur it out and then add text on top of that image instead, like this:
											- background-blurred
										- As far as tools to create these images
											- , I use Adobe Photoshop, but you could also use Gimp, Paint.net (windows only), or Pixlr.
											- And since publishing this post, many commenters have suggestedPicMonkey and Canva too!
											- Actually, here’s a post of 10 photoshop alternatives that are completely free to use.
											- There’s also a free tool I just found called Fotor, which has a tool specifically used for adding text onto images. If you’d like to upload your own images to Fotor, click on the “add photos” button on the right hand side. It took me a while to find this function.
										- And lastly, let’s quickly go over the size of the images and the process of uploading them to Facebook.
											- I typically create an image that is 403×403 pixels square, knowing that the image will not be cropped or cut off when it appears in people’s feeds. I upload the image first, and then add in my status update and link to my content from there.
											- According to Amy Porterfield, however, Facebook recently made a change giving preference to link-share posts. These types of posts actually show up more often in people’s feeds, and they use a different image size too.
											- When you paste a link directly into your Facebook status area first, you’ll see some content automatically populate, including the title of your post, a description of it (which you can edit), and a spot for an image that is more rectangular in size. You can click through and select an image that’s already in your post, or you can upload your own. This is where you’d upload a unique image with some text perfectly suited for your Facebook fans, if you don’t have one in your post already.
											- The size of this image should be 560 pixels wide by 292 pixels tall.
											- They are weird images sizes, for sure, but I hope this helps you figure this out a little better. Now that I’ve been doing this religiously after each post and podcast episode, the process is getting much quicker, and the results are definitely there.

						  #SalesFunnel AKA Customer Tunnel Machine
							- Meta
								- Maximising Lifetime Customer Value means you can be losing money on front-end because you'll gain profit by in the delayed later stages. later stage 
								  (e.g. paid #TrafficArbitrage or free #ContentMarketing costs won't get recuperated til after getting them to buy low-end product or getting them on email list).
								  
								  The longer and more effective your funnel, the more you can delay being profitable to the later stages. This allows you to massively increase your customer base.
								  
								  Simply put, the more effort you put into #SalesFunnel creation - the higher the Customer Lifetime Value.

													- OptimisePress $99, unsure if it can be nulled because elements require accessing their server
														- Cookie Drop.  So say someone goes to your page and signs up and then is taken to another page where they have access to your ebook, videos, worksheets, or whatever else you’re giving away for free.  You don’t ever want to them to see that initial page again.  Once they’re in your sales funnel you want them to only see the free content, and the sales offer which will also be inside. Optimize Press uses what’s called a cookie drop to place a cookie in your user’s computer which tells the site that they’ve already signed up and they don’t need to see the squeeze page anymore.  This is extremely useful for both you and them, and makes the process much easier.

														- Pros/Cons
															- Pros
																- Lots of content modules can be added to page
																  https://www.elegantthemes.com/preview/Divi/features/#modules
																- Allows split testing many page elements eg headline, optin form,
																- Easy to run separate development and production sites as you can import & export layouts easily to new sites
																- Easy to copy & paste layouts and elements onto new pages
																- Great video and image parallax backgrounds
																- Very customisable - content, colours, layout etc
																	- Also useful to ensure it remains responsive, looks good on mobile etc
																	- Disable elements for mobile or whatever visitors as needed
																	- Modules for Search box, Comments, Post Navigation (& limit to certain categories etc)
																- Beautiful landing pages - animations, flat icon design
																- Divi Leads - split-testing component
																  intralink2:: https://workflowy.com/#/4b3726173464
																- Live Preview any module instantly - desktop, mobile or tablet
																	- This may be even better than Thrive Content Builder full page version
																- Can be used on blog posts like Thrive
																- Global Library Elements e.g. elements, sections, rows, modules etc (create them once, use them across pages - sharing the same copy. And you can choose them to stay in sync, and selectively sync certain parts of the design
																- Content Library (create parts of pages, and re-use them easily in other pages)
																- Full revision control (so you can go back if you make a mistake)

																- Box colour meanings

																	- Purple = sections with full-width

																	- Page Settings
																	- Divi Leads
																		- Can split-test any element, not just entire pages
																		- Select the goal for a page
																		- Can also track off-page goals to allow tracking conversions throughout later stages of funnel
																			- which means you can track both on-page conversions while at the same time tracking conversions at the end of your sales funnel using our custom tracking shortcode.
																			- Using shortcode tracking setting
																- Design
																	- Responsive Editing Controls
																		- For every 'Advanced Design' setting you can change the values for Mobile/Tablet/Desktop
																	- Custom margins (to give blank space around a module)
																	- Custom padding (to increase the size of the module)
																	- Post Title Module - combine featured images with titles and text in various ways
																	- Post Formats
																		- Standard, Audio, Gallery, Quote, Video and Link post typesm
																- Divi Role Editor
																	- a new tool that can be used to limit/control every function within the Divi Builder for each of the WordPress user roles you are using (it works for custom user roles too). Assign your client a custom WordPress user role, and then open up the Divi Role Editor to control what the user is allowed to do.
																- Global Library Elements
																	- Use 'Save & Add to Library' to mirror it across multiple pages
																	- Section, Row or Module synced sitewide
																	- Selectively sync only certain tabs e.g. Advanced Design Settings so that design is maintained sitewide but content can be different on each
																	- Global Sidebars
																		- Can't create a Global Sidebar within Divi - the whole Section/Row must be Global, individual modules can't be non-Global

													- Thrive Content Builder
														- https://thrivethemes.com/contentbuilder
														- Pros
															- Makes blog post/page creation much easier as it does real WYSIWYG (front end page builder) - faster reloading + more accurate representation
															- No need to reload pages - updates automatically
															- Good for landing pages elements
															- Thrive Landing Pages (templates - component of Thrive Content Builder)
															  https://thrivethemes.com/landingpages/
															- Has click-to-trigger/two-step optin forms
														- Cons
															- No, unfortunately Thrive Content Builder can't be used to edit content that wasn't created with Thrive Content Builder.
																- This is necessary in order to ensure compatibility and avoid conflicts.​
																- You can, however, create and edit any new posts and pages with the Content Builder.
														- [downloaded in My Docs]
														- http://madlemmings.com/2016/01/04/divi-page-builder-vs-thrive-content-builder-vs-beaver-builder/#tab-con-6
														- http://www.bloggertipstricks.com/thrive-content-builder-review.html
													- Beaver Builder
														- Pros
															- Can uninstall the plugin and it not affect anything since it doesn't rely on shortcodes
																- Explanation
																	- However, Beaver Builder creates HTML so once you remove it, everything still works fine (doesn't rely on shortcodes)
																	- If I used Beaver Builder, it wouldn’t leave me in shortcode hell because it saves its changes to the wp_postmeta table in WordPress’ database, not the wp_posts table.
																	- Beaver Builder injects inline CSS styles. Divi injects a crap-ton of shortcodes.
																	- Email
																		- Thanks for reaching out with your question! Every time you make changes and publish a builder layout, a stripped down version of the layout is saved to the WordPress editor. So once the builder is removed, that stripped down version is what you will see.
																		- It won't look anything like it was before, but the content will be there, and will be readable. All styling and JS that is handled by the builder will be gone, which includes columns, row backgrounds, galleries, buttons, sliders, accordions, and etc. What's left will just be the contents that you have on the page.
																- Live Examples
																	- Just removes all formatting in section 4
																	  https://web.archive.org/web/20160417233915/http://benweiser.com/beaver-builder-review-wordpress-page-builder-live-hype/
														- Cons
															- Is avoiding shortcode hell really necessary? Divi is available in plugin form
														- Free lite plugin
														  https://wordpress.org/plugins/beaver-builder-lite-version/
														- Demo
														  http://benweiser.com/try-beaverbuilder
													- VelocityPage
														- http://velocitypage.com - find it nulled
														- Doesn't use shortcodes
														- Avoid ones which utilise shortcodes heavily e.g. Visual Composer
															- For
																- Though it's not a realistic scenario - if buying a custom theme, I have money to pay someone to fix old pages.
																- And why would you deactivate the plugin when you know you rely on it?
																- Plugin page builders mean I'm not locked into themes
															- Cons
																- pages are ruined if plugin uninstalled later
														- VelocityPage is a great plugin built by WordPress core contributor Mark Jaquith. Jaquith seems to not only understand the technical limitations of most WordPress themes and their landing page shortcomings, but he also knows what the most important tasks of a landing page are.
														- Therefore, you can enable this plugin on any page and then you get to choose whether or not to use your current theme as the base of the page, or switch to one of the templates that came with the plugin (the latter being a more landing-page-friendly option).
														- Features
															- Front-end editing with full drag-and-drop support
															- WooCommerce compatibility
															- Aweber and MailChimp opt-in forms
															- Mobile-optimized output
															- Possibility to use custom HTML
															- A number of predefined content blocks you can use on any page
															- Promises to work with 94% of all WordPress themes
														- Pros
															- One of the biggest pros here is that you get a selection of pre-made landing page templates together with the plugin. With these, you can quickly create things such as opt-in pages, landing pages for ebooks, pages for apps, pages for webinars, and more.
															- No coding or design skills required to use the plugin.
														- Cons
															- Despite the front-end editing feature being really useful, it doesn’t represent the exact form of the page you’re editing. It’s always good to switch to the live preview every once in a while.
															- It’s a bit pricey. The top-of-the-line Professional license (for use on unlimited sites) is $247.
														-
													- OptimisePress comes in plugin format too
														- Also optimise press has both theme and plugin forms now
														- $97 for 3 sites

											- Additional Elements

									- Types of Landing Pages
										- Lead Magnet Optin
										- Dedicated Newsletter Optin
										- Resource (Optin) Page
											- Traditional
												- Like landing pages with no sidebar
												- Just orients user towards most important content on site (consider opt-in forms + image navigation to resource page in sidebar)
												- These are called resource pages and while they operate similarly to traditional landing pages (in that they are distraction free/single column) there are a few other requirements to maximize their effectiveness.
												- 1.) The page should address an important topic on your blog
												- The first requirement of a resource page is that it needs to address an important topic that commonly appears on your blog.
												- Since Copyblogger is all about online marketing, having a resource page dedicated to a very important subject (landing pages) is a very smart thing to do.
												- It gives people a “starting point” if they are specifically interested on the topic of landing pages, it lets passerbys know that Copyblogger talks regularly about that subject, and it allows the Copyblogger editorial team to bring new life to some of the best content on the site that deals with landing pages.
												- Speaking of which…
												- 2.) The page should link to 3-10 pieces of your best content (on the subject)
												- Categories are a crappy way to allow people to find your best content, because they put things in chronological order rather than letting you put your best foot forward (I only use categories in WordPress for my own organization).
												- Resource pages, however, are going to be prominently featured on your site, will be visited often by people who want more on the topic, and give you the advantage of putting whatever posts fit best on that page.
												- If you scroll down on that Copyblogger page, you’ll find they give you 9 “getting started” articles that represent the best of what’s available on landing page information:
												- Now interested readers can really get to the good stuff on your site about a topic that they enjoy (a win for both of you).
												- But wait a minute, what’s this have to do with list building?
												- 3.) An opt-in box to get more info
												- est posts and other features, and since they serve as a “content hub” on a popular topic within your industry, these pages often rank very well in search engines.
											- Examples
												- Huge posts, which are basically eBooks in post form (easy to share + SEO benefit)
												  https://web.archive.org/web/20160419003101/https://www.helpscout.net/resources/
									- Sales Letter Formula
									  #SalesLetter
										- Sales Letter Formula
											- Draft
												- Research
													- Fact Sheet for product (red index box):
														- Note every feature e.g. length, facts about author (age, background, success stories etc) including 'negative' facts for objections
														- For each feature note all of it's BENEFITS i.e. what will product do for you, "so" in every line
														- Below benefits in italics note down objections
														- Find EXACT figures for claims
														- what are its problems and how he’s tried to cure them, why it’s better than its competitors, who likes it, who doesn’t like it, what proof he’s got that it works, what strange uses have people got out of it, what funny stories has he accumulated in regard to its manufacture or use, what problems was he trying to solve when he created it, how would he improve it if he had unlimited money, what causes most of his refunds, who works for him to help him make it, how it is made, how does he keep up the quality, who writes him what about it, etc.
														- Order according to importance and put stars next to important points (up to 3*s)
														- Unique Solution with Strong Benefits
														      How will I get rid of their pain?
														      Free trial? A huge and legitimate discount? Easy payments? 
														  
														  e.g. I'll Sell You This New Caddy For $18,000 And I'll Let You Drive It For A Week Free Before You Decide To Buy It... Plus, You Don't Have To Pay Me For It All At Once; You Can Pay Me Over A Period Of Five Years!
														  
														  Other parts of offer: 
														  **Guarantee
														  Add Bonuses** https://workflowy.com/#/5165174dfb61
															- List out at least 10 benefits
															- Bring out ALL your important benefits at once itemized (learn which are ones by testing headlines)
															- Find the benefits behind the benefits - e.g. titanium shaft on golf club (feature) > hitting the ball further (benefit) > admiration of friends after the golf game (benefit behind the benefit)
															- Don't ever sell a product - sell a solution to a problem
															- Hint about benefits from the very beginning + often
															- Flesh them out one-by-one
															- Link benefits to features for more credibility - e.g. more money allosws you to buy a new car for impressing friends + comfort/luxury, nice house, peace of mind, vacations, attracting opposite sex, leisure time
															- + secretely what they need (right before they're convinced of that you can provide what they want)
															- Build up incredible value (so that they feel stupid not to buy)
															- Backup the big promise headline with a quick explanation (with a subheadline
															- * they'll first create approximately 5 sub headings centered in the middle of the page under the Main Headline worded as questions. Questions which it's only possible in the subconscious mind to reply Yes! to.
														- Add Bonuses
														  Would you like a free gift with your purchase like a color TV or a toaster oven? A night on the town with Kim Bassinger or Paul Newman? A free Florida vacation?
														  
														          People will buy just for the volume of bonuses - have call-to-action, then bonuses list, then CTA, then even more testimonials etc
														          Personalised bonuses e.g. book with personalised cover (belongs to them)
														          e.g. personal coaching tips - give them a form to fill in and you'll return with personalised tips. Call it - coaching in your inbox/home/
														          offer free sample/send something on approval/buy first package i.e. no risk, cost or obligation
													- Audience Research
														- BUYER - habits, desires, language
														- What is the buyer's desire? What solution do they want, or already pay for?
														- e.g. demographics - age, income, nationality
														- psychographics - hobbies, things they read, beliefs
														- - imagine how they live their lives on an everyday moment-by-moment basis
														- What are their fears, who/why do they hate, daily frustrations, what do they desire most,
														- What trends are occurring and will occur in their businesses or lives?
														- Is there a built-in bias to the way they make decisions? (Example: engineers = exceptionally analytical)
														- Do they have their own language
														- Who else is selling something similar to them, and how?
														- Who else has tried selling them something similar, and how has that effort failed?
														- Use the product, talk to others who use it and analyse competitors products
														- What Is The Most Important Benefit to Your Reader?
														- Read their magazines, websites, forums, newsletters, go to events, mastermind with both other sellers to this group + group themselves, "play prospect" with other sellers
														- What’s the biggest problem facing that one person right now ?” Everything you say or write must focus on that. You must find their current motivation. You can't motivate someone INTO buying what you have. You simply have to channel their existing motivation TO your product or service. - why yours is the best solution
													- Competitors
														- Listen to them e.g. opportunities they see and are using, missing etc
														- Use copy from same niche e.g. list down all the benefits they note + link your features to their benefits, spice them up but don't hype it

													- Writing Headlines
													  #WritingHeadlines
														- **PRINCIPLES**
															- Desire-based
															  #Desire https://workflowy.com/#/af1ec707ac0d
															- Write at least 10 headlines
															- The purpose of the main headline is to get your readers really bloody excited about what you have to offer them.
															- Get Attention (Agitate)
																- Also make it Intriguing, Believable and Emotional
																- Specific emotional appeal - makes their life easier, faster, more convenient etc. in x days, saves x time etc
															- 4 U's
																- Useful eg makes their life easier, faster, more convenient etc. in x days, saves x time etc
																- Urgency
																- Unique (Benefit)
																	- Unsolved problem/solution + unfulfilled desire
																- Ultra-Specific
															- Forehead Slap Test
																- Would you wake up, slap yourself in the forehead and say I really need X-HEADLINE!
															- Intriguing/Believable/Emotional
																- News (revelatory info)
																- Challenge their beliefs e.g. x no longer works, x is good/bad for you
																- Curiosity/Mystery e.g. “Why Getting Arrested Made Me A Better Writer”
																- Challenge someone's beliefs e.g. x no longer works, x is good/bad for you
																- Piggybacking (famous brand/person)
															- Audience responds to Threats, Simple Solutions, Avoiding Mistakes, Lists
															- Offer hidden insight e.g. The secret to/keys to/crucial aspects/(One Method You Probably Don’t Know About)/The Real Reason Behind
															- Ask a question eg Is Commenting on Blogs a Smart Traffic Strategy? Do You Know Why Printer Ink is so Expensive? We Reveal the Truth
															- Write at least 30 emotionally descriptive headlines that mention the problem that you solve before choosing one.
															- Grab your readers by the throat your readers with your headline
															- http://www.smartgraphiccreator.com/HeadlineCreator.html or http://www.raimundasm.com/ultimate-graphic-creator/
														- EXAMPLES SWIPE FILE
															- [Something Complicated] Made Simple
																- WordPress Site Setup Made Simple
															- Oops! [# Topic] Mistakes & How To Avoid Them
																- Oops! 10 Common Blogging Mistakes & How To Avoid Them
															- [#] [Type of People] Reveal What Really [Does Something]
																- 14 Ebook Authors Reveal What Really Makes Their Ebook Sell
															- [#] Tricks to [Do Something] & [Do Something Else] Faster
																- 13 Tricks to Get Visitors & Convert Them Into Subscribers
															- How To [Do Something Cool]
																- How To Sell Your Products at a Higher Price
															- The Truth About [Something]
																- The Truth About Creating Great Blog Content
															- [#] Best [Tricks,Techniques,Tips] to Fuel/Boost Your [Something]
																- 64 Best Techniques to Boost Your Website Traffic
															- [Do Something] This Week [While Doing Something Else]
																- Get More Articles Written This Week Spending Less Time at Your Desk
															- The Ultimate [Something] Plan/Guide
																- The Ultimate Twitter Marketing Guide or The Ultimate Pinterest Marketing Guide
															- [#] Reasons Your [Something] Is Not [Doing What It's Suppose To Do]
																- 5 Reasons Your Blog’s Not Making Money
															- [Topic] Mistakes Even Smart People Make
																- Podcasting Mistakes Even Smart People Make
															- How Safe Is Your [Valuable Person/Object] from [Threat]?
																- 7 Warning Signs That [Blank]
																- Warning: [Blank]
																- Warning: Use [Blank] At Your Own Risk
																- Can We Really Trust [Person/Company/Product]?
																- The Shocking Truth about [Blank]
																- The Great [Blank] Hoax
																- How [Blank] Gamble with Your [Blank]: 7 Ways to Protect Yourself
																- [#] Lies [Group of People] Like to Tell
																- 13 Things Your [Trusted Person] Won't Tell You
																- 5 Little-Known Factors That Could Affect Your [Blank]
																- [Your Audience] Alert: The New [Blank] Scam to Avoid
															- The Zen of [Blank]
																- How to Take Charge of Your [Unruly Problem]
																- Can't Keep up? 11 Ways to Simplify Your [Blank]
																- The Minimalist Guide to [Aggravation]
																- 10 Shortcuts for [Completing Tedious Process] in Record Time
																- Get Rid of [Recurring Problem] Once and for All
																- How to End [Problem]
																- How to [Blank] in 5 Minutes
																- 101 [Blank] Hacks: A Cheat Sheet for [Blank]
																- How/Why [Blank] (And How To Stop/Beat/Avoid/What to do about it)
															- [Do Something] Like [Famous Person]: 20 Ways to [Blank]
																- [Famous Person’s] Top 10 Tips for [Blank]
																- The [World-Class Example] School of [Blank]
																- The [World-Class Example] Guide to [Blank]
																- Secrets of [Famous Group]
																- What [World-Class Example] Can Teach Us about [Blank]
															- Do You Make These 9 [Blank] Mistakes?
																- 7 [Blank] Mistakes That Make You Look Dumb
																- 5 [Blank] Mistakes That Make You [Look/Sound] Like a [Blank]
																- 11 [Blank] Mistakes You Don't Know You're Making
																- Don't Do These 12 Things When [Blank]
																- ["Quote”] admits [Famous Person]
															- How to [Benefit]
																- How to Be [Desirable Quality]
																- How to [Blank] (Even If [Common Obstacle])
																	- How to Be a Leader (Even if You're Naturally Shy)
																- How to [Blank] Without [Objectionable Action]
																- How to [Do Something] While You [Do Something Else]
																	- How to Get an MBA while You Commute to Work
																- How to [Benefit and [Benefit]
																	- [[calibre/How to Win Friends and Influence People - Dale Carnegie (2009)]]
																	- This headlines promises double the benefit.
																- How to [Do Something] That Your [Target Audience] Will Love
																- How to Use [Blank] to [Blank]
																- How to [Specific Benefit] in [Short Time]
																	- How to Lose 10 Pounds in 10 Days
																- How to [Blank] -- The Ultimate Guide
																- How to Be Smart in a World of Dumb [Group]
																- How You Can [Blank] In 3 [Days/Weeks/Months]
																- How to Get Grants that Pay for Your Business
																	- This one is nice because it sounds like you get the grant and the grant takes care of the rest.
																- How to Dress Like a Gentleman in 7 Easy Steps
																	- This makes the process sound manageable.
																- How to Meet Men without Using Dating Sites
																	- This headlines removes a potentially objectionable activity.
																- How to Drive the Golf Ball Further and Leave Your Friends inDisbelief
																- How to Escape the 9-to-5 and Fire Your Boss
																- How to Give Your Body a Relaxing Massage at a Tenth the Price
																- The Concept Artist’s Career Guide
																- Take the headline up a notch
																	- Good: How to Knit. Better: How to Knit a Scarf that Looks Like you bought it in a Designer Store
															- (Lists) 7 Ways to [Do Something]
																- 101 [Blank] for [Event/Cause/Process]
																- 72 [Killer/Timeless/Smart] Resources for [Audience/Process]
																- The Top 10 [Blank]
																- 7 [Blank] Secrets Every [Audience] Should Know
																- 7 Surprising Reasons [Blank]
																- The 5 Laws for [Blank]
																- 7 Steps to [Blank]
																- Get [Blank]! 10 Ideas That Really Work
																- 7 Things Your [Target Audience] Needs to Hear You Say
																- 56 Reasons Why [Blank]
															- Direct Headlines (news, an offer, etc)
																- Pure Silk Blouses – 30 Percent Off
																- Free SEO E-book
																- Introducing [Blank]
																- (Why) [Blank] No Longer Works
															- Indirect Headlines (often double meaning utilised)
																- Fresh Bait Works Best (about link bait)
															- Question Headlines (they must want to see it answered - controversial/emotional. Make them answer subject line in head)
																- Do You Close the Bathroom Door Even When You’re the Only One Home?
																- Who Else Wants to Get Rich Online?
																- [Blank]: Do you want the Good News or Bad News First?
																- Is [Blank] A Good Idea/Strategy?
																- Does [Blank] Really Matter?
															- Command Headlines
																- Put a Tiger in Your Tank
																- Subscribe to Copyblogger Today!
															- Secret Headlines
																- Don't Read This: [Blank]
																- The [Blank] That [Group] Don't Want You To See
																- Discover Why [Blank]
															- Interview Headlines
															- Finality) Ultimate Guide To [x]
															- Greatest [x]
															- 8 ways/tips/rules/]
															- ...that people want
															- killed/dead/mistakes/ultimate/definitive/simple/powerful/guide/habits/secret/*celebrity name*/*paradoxes/contrasts*/the #1/irresistible/killer/how to beat it/magnetic/do you recognise
															- Item: Hype e.g. Motivation: Massive and Long-Lasting
															- THEY DIDN’T THINK I COULD _______ , BUT I DID.
																- our natural tendency to root for the underdog. We’re fascinated by stories of people who overcome great obstacles and ridicule to achieve success. When this headline refers to something you have thought about doing, but talked yourself out of, you’ll want to know if the successful person shared your doubt or fear or handicap.
															- They Laughed When I Sat Down At The Piano—But Not When I Started To Play!
															- WHO ELSE WANTS _______ ?
																- implication that a lot ofother people know something that the reader doesn’t
															- Who Else Wants A Screen-Star Figure?
															- HOW _______ MADE ME _______ .
																- This headline introduces a first-person story
																- This headline structure seems to work best with dramatic differences.
															- How A “Fool Stunt” Made Me A Star Salesman
															- ARE YOU _______?
																- The question headline is used to grab attention by challenging, provoking, or arousing curiosity.
															- Examples:
																- ■ Are You Ashamed Of The Smells In Your House?
																- HOW I _______.
																- ■ How I Turned A Troubled Company Into A Personal Fortune.
																- IF YOU ARE _______, YOU CAN _______.
																- If You Are A Nondrinker, You Can Save 20% On Life Insurance.
																- SECRETS OF _______.
																- Secrets Of A Madison Ave. Maverick—“Contrarian Advertising.”
																- THOUSANDS (HUNDREDS, MILLIONS) NOW _______ EVEN
																- THOUGH THEY _______.
																- Two Million People Owe Their Health To This Idea Even Though
																- They Laughed At It.
																- WARNING: ________________.
																- ■ Warning: Two-Thirds Of The Middle Managers In Your Industry
																- Will Lose Their Jobs In The Next 36 Months.
																- GIVE ME _______ AND I’LL _______.
																- ■ Give Me Just 1 Hour A Day And I’ll Have You Speaking French
																- Like “Pierre” In One Month.
																- ■ Give Me A Chance To Ask Seven Questions And I’ll Prove You
																- Are Wasting A Small Fortune On Your Advertising.
																- _______ WAYS TO _______.
																- 101 Ways To Increase New Patient Flow.
																- Mention a Celebrity, Grab Attention
																- “If you have watched Oprah or the 6 o’clock news and wondered
																- where you can get the latest cosmetic surgery done, you need look no
																- further . . .”
