																- - especially blog titles from my niche
																- 1. Visit Google images -> Google Images
																- 2. Try Readers Digest on the search and there are a few ...
																- - 13 Things no one ever tells you about weight loss
																- - 13 Things your computer guy won't tell you
																- - 41 Things doctors never tell you
																- People don't tell you 13 (or 41) things about ___
																- - Gov't Ripoff : $1 Trillion of your money wasted
																- Think an accountant, business consultant or marketer can use that one?
																- - FATAL Hospital Mistakes You Can Avoid
																- Oooh chilling
																- - The MAGIC power of SLEEP
																- - How Doctor's Gamble With Your Life
																- This one can be adapted in so many ways eh?
																- - 6 Brilliant NEW Ways to Lose Weight
																- This one really made me chuckle
																- - Eat This, Lose Weight: The New Science Of Dieting
																- Hmmm ... Do This, Get More Customers: The New Easier Method Of Getting Tons Of Leads
																- Why stop at Readers Digest.
																- Let's go to some other good headline magazines.
																- Cosmopolitan has punchy numerical ones for a female audience and Men's Fitness has awesome headlines for a male audience - And ... you can see which ones were repeated a 100 times (Repeated again ... Know why?)
																- Want to have fun while you are finding them. Why not try "National Enquirer". If celebrity gossip is not your thing, then ...
																- The motherlode is "Weekly World News"
																- Should get you going eh?
																- Oh another good one ... Try "The Sun Newspaper" for more
													- Next:
														- Out will pop a central selling idea
															- May help to do an outline first
														- Now your "mental floodgates" will be wide open. Ideas will come gushing out like water from a broken fire hydrant. Capture those ideas. Forget form. Forget grammar or spelling. Write. Write. Write. Write. As fast as you can. Hurry! Hurry! Hurry! Hurry! Tell everything. Every detail. Every nuance. Every benefit. Every product feature. Every advantage.
															- Get it all down on paper, every persuasive idea and don't worry about length/grammar, etc
											- Restructure
												- Sales Triggers
													- Social Proof
													- Most Will Buy “bandwagon effect"
														- e.g. create a collage of emails/tweets/comments asking about product
														- creating the idea that a huge trend has developed, everybody is getting involved, and anyone who passes it up is, quite simply, an idiot.
														- ". . . thousands have joined in the last 30 days. Only a small number of people have received this invitation, and we fully expect most of them to take immediate advantage of this amazing discount—so if our phone lines are busy when you call, please be patient and keep trying. We have added extra customer-service people to handle everybody’s calls as rapidly as possible."
													- Ego Appeals - convey it as a status symbol
														- WHAT EXCUSE DO YOU MAKE WHEN ASKED FOR YOUR FAX NUMBER— AND YOU HAVEN’T GOT ONE? Can you afford to appear “behind the times” to your clients, customers, vendors, and associates? Or is it important to you to be perceived as successful, savvy, in tune with the trends leading the American business scene?
													- Give a warning
													- explain the pain of the experience by not accepting your offer
													- Contrast the world with problems and world without problems
														- LOSS AVERSION - benefits in terms of anti-vision. no dead end job, etc + gains. tell them vision and anti-vision
														- THEN make it clear that product/service will get them there
														- "You no longer need to be frustrated. The x indvidual, the one you know you're ebtter than, is using x that anyone cna implement"
														- ((67375e54-f32b-4657-a4cf-705f7a422cee)) > I'll tell you about that later
														- "But let me tell about my other friend, Steve, who xyz"
												- The Irresistible Offer
													- Call to Action
													  #CallToAction Urgency (quantity/time limit)
														- “Imagine your letter being read by a guy in an apartment in Cleveland, in the midst of a ferocious winter storm, with gusting winds and snow outside at thigh height. You’ve got to get him so excited that he’ll get out of the chair in front of the fireplace, bundle up, slog through the snow, go out to his cold car, and drive down to the post office to get a money order and a stamp to send his order in—rather than take the risk of waiting until tomorrow.”
														- Tell him what he will get if he hurries and tell him what he will lose if he delays.
														- 1. Limited Availability
															- If you are honestly making an offer in which either the primary product or the premium or a discount or rebate is limited by availability, you can try to convince the recipient that “the race is on!”
														- 2. Premiums
															- It is rare for the basic offer to be strong enough in and of itself to inspire immediate response from a satisfactory number of people. Because of this, I am a strong advocate of using premiums, and usually prefer a premium over a discount or rebate.
															- e.g. better product available at same price, but only 1000 available
															- - make the premium exciting and desirable
															- - believable urgency-building story
															- FREE TV AND VCR—BUT YOU MUST CALL NOW! Now you can get one of our newest systems—plus a free TV/VCR. But you must act immediately. We have only 250 of these TV/VCRs and cannot obtain any more.
															- I am writing to just a fraction of my previous guests for this first time offer. This is a test and may never be repeated again. But please remember, this is a test and is being offered to only a fraction of our previous guests and may never be offered again. This offer is available only until Thursday, November 1st, or until our allotted number of Hawaiian vacations is gone, whichever comes first, so I urge you to act quickly.
														- 3. Deadlines
														- 4. Multiple Premiums
															- - if premium works, test a double premium
															- The company offered one cabinet with an $X order THEN added two cabinets with a larger $Y order.
															- i.e. bonuses with tiered packages
														- 5. Discounts for Fast Response; Penalties for Slow Response
															- ENROLL BY JANUARY 15: $149.00 per person ENROLL AFTER JANUARY 15 BUT BEFORE FEBRUARY 20: $199.00 per person AT THE DOOR (if available): $229.00 per person
															- could be applied to advance-order offers tied to new, soon-to-be-released products; any kind of event tickets or passes; subscriptions or subscription renewals; and other offers
														- 6. Sweepstakes and Contests
														- Scarcity
															- You Can Buy Only If . . .

																- Many use an “application process,” to make people qualify to buy. But few of them have gone to the extremes one did. At various times, he required prospective purchasers to listen to 7 hours of introductory material and sign an official-looking affidavit attesting that they had done so before they were permitted to buy.
																- if you think you can qualify, you’ll have to complete a detailed questionnaire, which will be reviewed by our Advisory Committee. Only if you are approved at that stage will you be invited to come to the Home Office for a personal interview.

														  Last, But Not Least
														  Finally, I want you to do one thing. It’s simple, and shouldn’t take you very long. If you’ve read this far, take a second and write out in the comments section the best advice (in one sentence, preferably) you have for a beginning blogger trying to get started submitting guest posts. It can be from this list or your own—let’s get a good value-adding discussion going!
														  Also, be sure to say hello on Twitter!
														  
														  ---
														  Your Turn
														  It’s that time again: time for you to do work!
														  Let me know what you thought about these tactics & examples.
														  Do you have any other tips on emailing busy people?
														  Thanks for reading, please share this article if you enjoyed it.
														  -----
														  
														  What else would you do?
														  These are 5 simple ways to promote your guest post and get the most out of it. Remember that promoting your guest posts and doing all these steps helps you to become a better guest poster, but not only that. It also helps your site because at the end you’ll get the backlink with higher PR juice. And finally, but not less important, it helps the Blog to grow with targeted content.
														  The owner will love your post and will want more of you. That helps building a relationship that in the future could help you a lot too.
														  In a world where good guest bloggers are hard to get (most of them don’t even read the guidelines and send horrible pitches), you’re going to be 1 in a million. It’s a win-win situation.
														  What else would you do after your guest post has been published?
														  
														  
														  -----
														  
														  So What Do You Think?
														  
														  If you’re subscribed to any newsletters, what kinds of emails make the most impact on you? What kinds of things do you like to read about?
														  
														  Also, am I crazy for using a filter email? I think I may be the only one at the moment who does has one. I’m interested in what you have to say.
														  
														  Thanks for your support everyone! SPI is growing so fast, thanks to you! Cheers!

														  If Only Offering 1 Tier Product:

														  What justifies this price? - Compare it to how much money they will make or time they will save
															- ((644c0a7b-fea6-458e-93d3-33bcf1166ef0))
															- 1. Soften the Pricing
																- Your pricing table should show the price for each plan, but don’t make it obvious to users that they’re about to spend a lot of money by emphasizing the prices. Instead, soften the pricing by putting more emphasis on the benefits and features for each plan. The more users focus on the benefits and features, the more value they’ll feel like they’ll get from you if they sign up. To soften the pricing even more, avoid adding cents to your prices. Adding in cents adds more numbers to the price and makes it feel like they’re going to spend a lot of money.
																- https://app.asana.com/0/13939091628542/30737471560169/f
															- 2. Add a Higher Pricing Plan as a Decoy (so they think they're not spending that much)
															- Place Them in Descending Order of Price (bracketing/anchoring)
															- 4. Highlight the Middle Plan
																- Most users will often end up choosing a plan that’s not too expensive or cheap. Make it easy for users to decide which pricing plan they should choose by highlighting one in the middle. By doing this, users will focus on the one you highlight and consider it more. Show users that it’s the most popular plan by making it standout head and shoulders above the rest and it will look like the most attractive choice.
															- 5. Use Strikethroughs for Absent Features
															- 6. Downplay the Free Plan
																- Almost every web application will offer a free plan for users to try. However, not every pricing plan will make this glaringly visible. Downplay the free plan and you might find more users choosing between your paid plans. However, you should never downplay your 30-day free trials. Users want to try your application before they buy it. Because of that, all pricing plans should come with 30-day free trials. The benefit of deflecting users from your free plan is that when the free trial period ends, users have to decide with their credit card whether they want to cancel or continue using your service.
																- https://app.asana.com/0/13939091628542/30737471560181/f
															- 7. Quantify Feature Power with Numbers and Unlimited
																- When users compare pricing plans, seeing a plan that’s limited in feature power next to a plan with unlimited feature power is quite compelling, especially if the price difference isn’t much. Unlimited is a word that conveys freedom, power and flexibility. To give users no limits to what they can do with your features is a selling point you can take advantage of. Give your higher pricing plans unlimited feature power, while limiting the feature power of your lower pricing plans, and you’ll make your higher pricing plans that much more attractive.
																- https://app.asana.com/0/13939091628542/30737471560175/f

												- Destroy Objections
												  Eliminate perceived negatives
													- Demolish the five basic objections within your copy:
														- A. I don't have enough time.
														- B. I don't have enough money. - can you afford the solution
														- C. It won't work for me.
														- D. I don't believe you. - is this the right solution for your situation
														- E. I don't need it. - is the problem important enough to solve it now
													- Address objections such as commonly faced problems, how they can take core product into more value,
														- Address every possible objection, concern, fear, doubt, excuse, question openly with Damaging Admission copy
														- Dialog / rhetorical questions great for handling objections (narrate their thoughts in italic?)
														- the answers to most objections or questions should include most of these items—and in most instances, all of them:
															- 1. A direct answer
															- 2. A verifying testimonial comment, case history, or story
															- 3. A restatement of or reference to the guarantee/free trial offer
														- - can be used to simultaneously remove the people you're not looking for
															- prove product is easy to use - "instant" transfer rather than learn, teach etc
														- - price is an indicator of value
															- people want to feel like they're getting a bargain, not that they're being cheap - they want to feel extravagant
															- Don't give away your package for free - cheapens the product rather than increasing value perception
															- - free is a lot different psychologically than even paying $1
															- - similar with sampling - get them to ask for a sample rather than giving it freely
															- Sometimes a price must be decided. A high price creates resistance. It is a well-known fact that the greatest profits are made on great volume at small profit.
															- But on other lines high price is unimportant. High profit is essential. The line may have a small sale per customer. A product which costs more than the ordinary is considered above the ordinary.
														- “draining the objections,
															- in which salespeople list the objections on a pad before answering any. They keep asking “Anything else?” until the customer runs dry of objections. Then they ask, “If we can take care of all these concerns to your satisfaction—and I’m not sure that we can—but if we can, you will then want to go ahead with the XYZ tonight, right?” When the customer says “yes” to that, he or she is boxed in. There’s no way I’ve found to duplicate that process in print.
															- "There are a few objections people tend to come up with when I tell them this offer. They say 1) xx2) xx 3) xxx
															- As attractive as this product/service/offer is, our marketing experts tell us that only about X% of the people receiving it will respond. Although that’s okay with us from a business standpoint, it still bothers me personally. You see, I know how much the owners/users of our product/service/offer benefit from it. I read their letters; I talk to them on the phone; I see them personally when they visit us; and hundreds/thousands/millions each year tell me that “(strong, brief customer quote).” Because of this, I just hate the thought of someone not getting our product/service/offer because of some error or omission in our explanation. That’s why I held a special brainstorming session with a group of our people just to try to figure out why you might say “no” to our free trial offer. After several hours, our group could think of only three possible reasons. Here they are:
														- Competitors
															- challenge them to try competitors, comment on their previous experiences but DON'T SPECIFICALLY MENTION COMPANIES
															- Shows sore loser behaviour if I try to disparage others
																- To attack a rival is never good advertising. Don't point out others' faults. It looks unfair, not sporty.
															- Competition must be considered. What are the forces against you? What have they in price or quality or claims to weigh against your appeal? What have you to win trade against them? What have you to hold trade against them when you get it? How strongly are your rivals entrenched?
															- Need to offer something exclusive - some impressive advantage to get them to change their habits
													- What are at least ten objections that they will or might have
														- What concerns/questions/objections might they have about the solutions?
												- FAQ
													- Don’t leave any of your user’s questions unanswered. A simple and concise FAQ at the bottom of your pricing table that covers the most common questions can make users feel comfortable signing up for a plan. For uncommon questions, send them to your contact page.
													- Go over objections again + guarantee + urgent call to action
												- P.S.
													- If your content is part of a sales letter, be sure to add a signature. If your content is more formal, consider adding a signature or bio box, even if it’s on your own website
													- DO close with several P.S. (P.P.S., P.P.P.S., etc) notes. Most people just skip to the end of long form sales letters. Good post scripts get your reader to actually go back over the body content of the letter to soak up the key information.
													- Always use a powerful P.S. that reiterates your entire message in just a few punchy sentences.

											- Editing
												- The 7 Rewrites
												  #Rewrite
													- Strategy
														- Repeat the Promise Continuously
															- Beginning - Tell them what you're going to tell them (get the key benefits out)
															- Middle - Tell them in a few different ways
															- In a straightforward statement + an example + a story, sometimes called a “slice of life” + testimonials + quote from a customer, expert, or other spokesperson
															- Conclusion - tell them what you've told them eg numbered summary, FAQ
														- Backup the Big Promise
															- "Yes" ladder - eg 5 questions/statements/cold reads they will subsconsciously say "Yes!" and nod to (best at beginning)
															- Also can start or finish paragraphs with Yes Questions, or use them as subheads
													- Unquestionable Credibility
														- Explain why you're making the offer.
														- (Sometimes) Click to tweet quote before a subhead

														- Convey the promise repeatedly and show the strongest one first
														- Power of because - Use good justification techniques, especially if a higher priced item, product or service.
														- Social Proof
														  #SocialProof
															- Use authority quotes (e.g. media, bloggers, famous quotes, tweets, reviews etc) nofollow links
															- Awards, achievements
															- Quote popular books + lessons learned
															- Testimonials - especially authorities and unique video testimonials
															- Case studies
																- specific results e.g. even though I run an x business, I was able to use X product to help me close 20% more sales
																- + photos e.g. before/after results or just of their face
														- USP / unique angle
														- Evidence
														  #Evidence
															- Tell only the instantly verifiable Truth (must be Unquestionable Credibility)
															- Link all claims to:
																- Anecdotes
																- Scientific evidence + neuroscience
																- Statistics, facts and correlations
																- Historical anecdotes or ancient tales
																- Logical analogues in life e.g. in nature same happens in X scenario or in everyday modern life (join the dots)
																	- Metaphors, similes, and analogies that relate to things generally accepted as true
																- Logical fallacies
																	- "Accident" used by Plato...eg. "You say you have never met this person. Can you be sure he was never near you in a sports crowd, for example?"
																	- "Consequential affirmation" (Placing the cart before the horse)..eg. "If I drop an egg, it breaks. This egg is broken, so I must have dropped it."
																	- "Bifurcation" (Black and White)...eg. "There are two types of people in this world: the  rich and the suckers. Do you want to get rich, or are you happy to remain a sucker?"
																	- "Plurium Interrogationum" (The one-answer qns)...eg. "Have you stopped stealing the cookies?" If you answer "yes", you admit stealing the cookies. If you answer "no", you still admit the same thing.
																	- Winning Every Argument ebook/how to win any argument/logical fallacies wiki
																- Comparisons
																	- With others conclusions, with competitors products (lead the conclusion, don't get spiteful)
																- 10+ years of research [their personal research] and tests and 30,000+ students backing up their claims that their content works.
																  Ramit Sethi
															- Answer objections
																- About content itself
																- and why to buy from our brand too Eg experience, caring and community etc
														- Use Specific Facts Over General Descriptions
															- General statements e.g. "prices have been reduced" means nothing, if you say "25% reduction" that means something

													- Readability
														- One main point per post
														- Use proofreader button
														- Shorten
															- Wordpress SEO plugin - Flesch Kincaid Reading Ease >60
															- Paragraphs - 3 sentences max
															- Sentences
															  Not all sentences though or you'll sound retarded.
																- Reducing Clauses to Phrases, Phrases to Single Words
																- Don't say the same thing twice.
																- Remove intensifiers e.g. really, very, quite
																- Explode a compound sentence into two sentences (you can start with 'but/and' etc)
																- Avoid expletive constructions e.g. there is/are or it is
																- Remove all words which add nothing
															- Words (max 5 char per word, otherwise find a synonym?)
															- Aggressively cutting out every word or phrase that fails to advance, strengthen, or reinforce your basic sales story
															- Convert or Die - gun to head. Every word in beginning must do it's job of getting you to read the next word.
															- Body - 3-5 sections, 100-200 words per section
															- Provoke, Don't Solve
																- Don't handle every objection in one sitting - have them ask more about features of product, challenges assumptions about a competitor, etc
																- Ask more questions than you answer so they discuss more in the comments
														- Simplify
															- Write for the beginner, not the expert
															- Summarise - join the dots for them
															- Storytelling
																- Introduce the idea with a concrete story (so simple, active sentences + emotional)
																- Use interesting stories to tell boring/abstract principles (like Freakonomics)
															- Clarity - define or remove all unnecessary phrases and jargon
															- But have some Brand Words

															- Eliminate Vague Modifiers (adjectives)
																- e.g. very, little, quickly, rather, basically, sometimes, finest, consistently, keen, strong, superior, smoothly, logical, cost-effective,
																- Focus instead on specific facts and benefits
														- Conversational Style
															- Vary tempo from short, punchy sentences to longer for contrast (for attention)
															- Slang, one word exclamations, rhetorical questions, etc

																- Bond, do you understand what I am saying? You do. Good, then let’s go on. Etc.
																- How do we get the benefits? The answer is simple. All we have to do is etc., etc. And so on.
																- Can you imagine that? I know it seems unbelievable but facts are facts! Etc., etc., etc. Onward.
																- Well, kiddo, it's past the lunch hour but better late than never, eh? Whatever. I'm stuck for a theme again. What do you suggest? O.K. How about the subject of IMPACT? Good, I'm glad you agree.
														- Dual Readership Path
														  Analysers & Skimmers
															- Skimmers need big, bold headlines and subheads; photos with captions; and boxed, circled, or highlighted short paragraphs
															- Analysers want long copy with lots of facts, figures, statistics, charts, graphs
															- Use centered subheads like D&P for skimmers, main content for analysers
															- Inverted Pyramid style
																- Paragraph structure:
																	- Stating your conclusion first, then supporting it with the sentences that follow.
																	- This helps scanners to move from point to point, and decide where they’d like to dive in deeper.
																	- Conclusion at start of paragraph/section, supporting evidence after (useful for drawing in skimmers).
														- Yes Questions
															- Get the reader to nod "yes!" to your copy with your questions + cold reads.
															- Beginning
															- Start or end of paragraphs or Subheads
														- Formatting elements
															- Parenthesis (like this) can provide "eye relief" for your reader
															- Bold - each point bold or use H2
															- Boxes esp Johnson boxes. - Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia
															- Bullets ■ ■ ■ (especially custom bullets)
															- Numbering: 1, 2, 3, and so on
															- Interactive action elements e.g. check boxes, fields, and text area's for polls, hyperlinks
														- Read aloud (View Post)
													- Formatting
														- Capitalise Every First Letter In Subheads
														- Images
															- According to a new study on “truthiness,” researchers found that images helped increase trust, even when the images were nonsensical or had nothing to do with the fact. http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22869334
															- Tips
																- Include a picture with eyes. So if you need attention on something on the page, let the eyes lead the way.
																- picturing men whom others envy, in surroundings which others covet. The pictures subtly suggest that these articles of apparel will aid men to those desired positions.
																- drawings, charts, graphs, and testimonials, all presented in a graphically interesting way
																- captions are an excellent addition. http://www.copyblogger.com/image-captions/
																- use photos over drawings and illustrations
																- Do nothing to merely interest, amuse, or attract. Eccentric pictures take away power from your copy (supporting > distracting pics)
																- Show, don't just tell - Use Jing to add images or screenshots to enhance the tutorial
																- Make pics unclickable (remove hyperlink)
																- Use supporting images, not attention grabbing images (the focus should be on the content, not the images)
																- Desirable people living desirable lives
																- Include a picture with eyes (people follow to see what they're lookingat)
																- Add drop shadow
																- Captions
																	- Use deep captions.
																	- Studies have shown that image captions are consistently some of the most-read copy on a page. Try pairing a strong image with a “deep caption.”
																	- Deep captions are two to three sentences long. That’s long enough to intrigue your reader to dig in to your whole article.

														- _Sales Letter ONLY:_
															- Graphic Enhancement
																- Design
																	- Johnson boxes. Use them. Johnson Box - Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia
																	- Use custom bullets for lists. Things like money bags, hypnotic circles, or bananas. Whatever goes with what you're selling.
																	- Try using "action elements" like check boxes, fields, and text area's for the user to take part in the sales letter.
																	- don’t want to underline, because underlining typically signals a link, so you can easily confuse people. Italics are hard to read.
																	- But I use a lot of boldface type, yellow highlighter marks, screened boxes, bullets, and color, as well as photos and illustrations. I even mark up letters as if I’d done it in handwriting!”
																- DON’T include a lot of distracting pictures in your sales letter. Only use images that draw attention to key text (e.g. arrows, “warning” signs, boxes, etc). Including your corporate logo or some random “cute” image will only distract your readers from what matters: the message.
														- _Blog post ONLY:_
															- Read More tag

																	  <blockquote class="twitter-tweet" data-partner="tweetdeck"><p>This post is full of win. <a href="https://twitter.com/PatFlynn">@PatFlynn</a>: Here are five 5-minute or less blogging tips that will yield BIG results! <a href="http://t.co/r57yPD1EeE">http://t.co/r57yPD1EeE</a></p>
																	  <p>— kevinmulligan (@kevinmulligan) <a href="https://twitter.com/kevinmulligan/statuses/433328894163156992">February 11, 2014</a></p></blockquote>
																	  <p><script charset="utf-8" type="text/javascript" src="//platform.twitter.com/widgets.js" async=""></script>

													- SEO
														- Yoast SEO
														- Interlink to related posts
															- https://app.asana.com/0/13939091628542/29788827709978/f
														- Link out to influencers (where appropriate) - though want to keep a spiderweb to keep visitors mostly onsite
														- Put main topics into google adwords kw tool/market samurai, select most popular wording
														- Cousin keywords density
													- Quality Checks
													  Spend an insane amount of time on detail
														- If this was the first post a visitor saw, what would they think?
														- Would you tell your son to buy from this man after having read this page?
														- Would people find this valuable (make this decision) with all the information and motivation in the world?
														- Would I say these things to sell in person?
														- What would be the customer's thought process towards each sentence? (often skeptical)
														- Minimum 10 revisions
												- Break-time
												  3-5 days to win back some objectivity
												- Final Review
													- Compare draft against examples to isolate the strengths and weaknesses of your words.
													- Read the Letter to Several People Who Might Be Typical Customers for the Offer
													- Get Second Opinions - join a mastermind with internet marketers

								- Optin Sequence
								  collapsed:: true
									- Activation Page
										- Ramit Sethi's
										  [picture of Ramit pulling a funny sad face] Caption: This is me Ramit Sethi. I'm sad. + 5:00 countdown timer on right side
										  Why?
										  
										  Because you haven't confirmed your subscription to my free newsletter by clicking the link in your inbox.
										  
										  That means I can't send you...
										  
										      My word-for-word guide on how to negotiate your salary
										      The exact scripts to lower your bank fees and credit card fees
										      My day-by-day roadmap for earning more money on the side
										  
										  I don't want to be sad any more.
										  
										  I want to show you how to earn more...and beat the banks...and pay off your debt right now.
										  
										  So go to your inbox and click the confirmation link that I just sent you. That way, I can send you my best material for free...and we can both be happy :)
										  
										  Your friend,
										  -Ramit
										  
										  P.S. Don't wait. 80% of people who confirm do so in the first 5 minutes -- and the rest never see my best material.
									- Confirmation/Thank You Page
										- Ramit's
										  aaron, you're done!
										  Congratulations, you're in!
										  We're building your page full of bonuses right now.
										  
										  Hang tight  it should take about 10 seconds, and you'll be redirected automatically...
										  
										  - Ramit
										  
										  Did the bonus page not load automatically?
										  Just don't like waiting?
										  
										  We're waiting for you, just click here to continue.
							- Email Marketing
							  id:: 644c0a79-0d6c-4add-bac7-198ba4ce278c
							  collapsed:: true
							  #EmailMarketing
								- _Software_
								  collapsed:: true
									- Ideal Features
									  collapsed:: true
										- _Generally 'Marketing Automation-only features_
										  collapsed:: true
											- Automated Emails based on all actions
											  Potential Sequences https://workflowy.com/#/5a60df910623
											- List Segmentation
											  collapsed:: true
												- The ability to segment your subscribers and send offers targeted to who they are and what they’ve done.
												- Tag How Customer Entered List (CRM)
												- Create custom subscriber lists using just about any criteria?
												- Prune your list of inactive subscribers?
											- Web Analytics
											  collapsed:: true
											  Integrated with lead scoring
												- track actions your subscribers take inside your emails and on the web
												- In order to determine how likely they are to buy. For example, if someone downloads 3 reports and attended a webinar, they are more likely to buy than someone who only signed up for blog updates.
												- _Related: _Conversion Optimisation - Analytics Tools
												  #ConversionRateOptimisation https://workflowy.com/#/18d9367caba4
											- Lead Scoring
											  collapsed:: true
												- Provides a score, often 1-100, on how ready a person is to make a purchase. In high-touch sales, higher scoring leads are typically assigned to sales agents who make phone calls and send one-to-one emails.
											- CRM
											  collapsed:: true
											  personalized lead nurturing
												- Customer Relationship Management which, contrary to it’s name, typically has nothing to do with “customers” and everything to do with following up and closing a sale.
												- Shows each customer individually and records all interactions
											- Visual workflows with timers and if-then actions
											- Global emails
											  collapsed:: true
												- Intro email or series to everyone

											- Sendy (works with [Amazon SES](https://workflowy.com/#/1fa10d47e274))
											  collapsed:: true
											  #EmailMarketing-Sendy
												- Cost
												  collapsed:: true
													- I pay $6/month for a server with Sendy on it, $60 one time fee for Sendy and email costs are negligible
													- The devs can set it up for you for $80
												- Features
												  collapsed:: true
													- Cost
													  collapsed:: true
														- £43 for Sendy software
														- Amazon SES emails e.g. $1 for 10k emails
														- $6/month DigitalOcean VPS (which can be used for multiple purposes)
													- Uses Amazon SES as a SMTP relay
													  collapsed:: true
														- Other major SMTPs include Mandrill, SendGrid and MailGun.
												- SOP
												  collapsed:: true
													- Setup
													  collapsed:: true
														- Choose host
														  collapsed:: true
															- Just be careful on your hosting. Sendy needs to have 3 cron jobs setup, and the autoresponder cron has to run every minute. Many hosts, like Hostgator, do not allow 1 minute crons on a shared hosting account. If you do it, they'll automatically come in and change it to a 23 hour cron which royally messes up your autoresponder. So make sure your host allows 1 minute crons OR else use a external cron service which is supported by Sendy.
															- External CRON service if VPS host does not allow 1 minute CRONs
															- Ideally use DigitalOcean
														- DigitalOcean - create a droplet with LAMP
														- Hostgator - set A record (Domain Forwarding)
														  collapsed:: true
															- sendy.habitlaboratory.com
															- On Hostgator cPanel > Domains > Simple DNS > A record: sendy + IP address of DigitalOcean droplet
														- Follow these instructions to setup DigitalOcean droplet
														  collapsed:: true
															- http://davekiss.com/create-your-own-mailchimp-clone-for-5-a-month-with-sendy/
															- Also saved in Sendy folder
														- Continue to step 4
														  collapsed:: true
															- service apache2 restart
															- Goto sendy.habitlaboratory.com and complete setup

											- Suggested Dedicated SMTP Providers
											  collapsed:: true
											  https://docs.woocommerce.com/document/email-faq/#section-5
												- There are dedicated SMTP providers that will work for most websites. All have their own plugin on WordPress.org and can be installed from your WordPress dashboard, and have support available to help you get started.
												- SendGrid (Plugin) – Send 12,000 emails free per month.
												- Mailgun (Plugin) – Send 10,000 emails free per month.
												- SparkPost (Plugin) – Send 100,000 emails free per month.
												- Mandrill (Plugin) – From MailChimp as a paid add-on. It costs $10/month for up to 25,000 emails. Emails are tracked and tagged for stats in the Mandrill Dashboard, and it can be used with MailChimp.
											- SenderScore by ReturnPath
											  collapsed:: true
												- Check and monitor the reputation of your websites IP address for sending emails, if you have your own dedicated server for sending emails. You can also use this for shared and virtual hosting environments, but it is better to get a dedicated SMTP provider instead
												  collapsed:: true
													- because those environments are not recommended for sending emails.
													  http://documentation.mailgun.com/best_practices.html#hosting
												- Visit Senderscore.org to learn more.
											- https://wordpress.org/plugins/postman-smtp/

												- ORBTR
												  collapsed:: true
												  $99/mo
													- It packs a powerful marketing automation punch, giving WordPress even more power. The plugin offers landing pages, lead tracking, segmentation, email marketing and few other features.
													- Few things you need to keep in mind. ORBTR does not offer built-in email functionality, instead it integrates with Mailchimp to send emails and collect data. Plus, to add forms to your landing pages you will need Gravity Forms plugin (or Jetpack forms). Compared to HubSpot, it is much more affordable. The monthly price covers support, upgrades and access to their analytics API.
													- ORBTR team offers a free lead tracking plugin. So even if you don’t buy subscription, do check out the free plugin.
													- Price: $99/mo

It offers similar features to what you would find in HubSpot’s Content Optimization System. It includes SEO advice and easy image insertion and attribution.
													- When it comes to automation, instead of having a built-in email marketing platform Spokal has comprehensive integration with ActiveCampaign for conditional flows. Plus, it offers SEO tracking and other reporting functionality one would need to get a comprehensive view of what is happening.

													- An Overview of the Workflow
													  collapsed:: true
														- This is where things get interesting! Let’s walk through how Mautic can be used. Imagine a new visitor comes to your website. They are an IP address, nothing more, maybe some basic geographic data. They browse pages on your website, eventually landing on a special page with a call-to-action form. This form collects a few bits of information (e.g. name and email address). Now this visitor is now a known visitor. You may have a lead list setup to capture these visitors and add them to a specific campaign. These campaigns will send emails, post to social media, and other actions based on either specific times or user interaction. Think of it like a decision tree. If the user opens the email, do this, otherwise do that. These campaigns can span hours, days, weeks, or even months!
														- Now are you starting to see what’s happening? This known visitor is gradually becoming a lead. As this lead completes various actions, you give them points. These points help you to weight your leads and see which leads are hot. Once a lead earns a certain amount of points they are automatically moved into your CRM or other system. Now you have a list of leads you can confidently know are ready to be directly contacted.

																	- Install the Mautic tracking code or pixel on the WP site which it seems like you already have. All new visitors will automatically be created as anonymous contacts in Mautic at that point, you just have to type "is:anonymous" in the filter on the contacts page or click the lock icon on the right side to view them. The WP Mautic plugin is great because it automatically embeds the tracking code and provides shortcodes for embedding Mautic forms, dynamic content, and gated videos.

															- Bloom integration
															  collapsed:: true
																- Bloom to use Mautic: http://techmotely.com/how-to-customize-mautic-form-to-work-with-bloom-plugin-and-make-it-look-like-the-image-below/
																- In a nutshell: 1.) Create a form in Mautic before you create it in Bloom. Turn off "Render Style." Set up fields, such as Name and Email. Save and manually grab the html code (there's two sections of code).
																- 2.) Then create a new optin form in Bloom. Select "Custom HTML Form" in the Form Integration section. Then add the html code you got from the saved Mautic form (second section of code from Mautic).
																- 3.) Add the first section of html code to your document page. If you use Elegant Theme's Divi Theme, go into Divi options and place it in Integrations.
																- I still have some problems with the fieldss (name, email, sign up button) looking wonky. I think I might have to fiddle with the Attribute tab in the Form Fields in Mautic.

														- Marketing elements
														  collapsed:: true
															- Create a New List
															  collapsed:: true
																- Click Leads > Manage Lists > New and you create a new list. In the example below, we'll store the details of everyone who signed up for our marketing.
																- Click Leads > Manage Fields and you control the information you collect from them
															- Create a New Campaign
															  collapsed:: true
															  Click Email > Manage Campaigns > New
																- The first step in creating your campaign is identifying the purpose and goal you want to accomplish.
																  collapsed:: true
																	- Maybe it’s introducing your leads to a new product, or simply keeping in touch through a weekly newsletter.

																  collapsed:: true
																  Drag-and-drop

																	- Drag-and-drop

																- Form builder
																- Landing page builder
																- Scoring leads: Points
															- Step 2. Target A Lead List
															  collapsed:: true
																- Once you decide the purpose of your campaign and the goals to accomplish the next step is to select the list of leads you want to target with this campaign. New leads can be added a list at any point in time.
															- Step 3. Identify Your Actions
															  collapsed:: true
																- One of the key parts of your campaign is to identify and create the actions you want to take during the campaign. These might include sending an email, posting to social media, or adding points to a lead based on decisions they make.
															- Step 4. Define Your Outcomes
															  collapsed:: true
																- Outcomes are the results of decisions made by your leads targeted by the campaign along with any outcomes you manually define. Outcomes include moving a lead to a new list, passing that lead to an integration, or creating another follow-up with that lead.

											- X2Engine
											  collapsed:: true
											  Open-source version and paid $12-65/month version
												- X2Engine is a Customer Relationship Management platform, which includes robust marketing automation features. One of the interesting aspects of X2Engine is that it is open source, and includes a free basic version. However, the free version doesn’t really have the marketing automation features one needs.
												- This platform comes in two different flavors and pricing, cloud and self-hosted. Talking to a friend who has more experience with X2Engine made me realize that this platform is not for the faint of hearts. This is especially true when it comes to the self-hosted version, it requires quite a bit of experience. If you decide to play with X2Engine, do go with cloud version and focus on learning how to use it effectively.

								- Potential Sequences
								  collapsed:: true
									- Have both Weekly & Daily lists
									   You email your channel list and also I recommend you have a weekly list. So I email my list with “Here’s your SEO video this week.” In the bottom of the email, you’ll notice it says, “If you prefer to get a weekly digest, click here.” If they click that link, it takes them off the daily broadcast and puts them onto a weekly broadcast. So they’ll only get a weekly email from me. Even if they’re on all my list, they should just get one weekly email. They’ll get a little digest of 3 – 5 posts that we put out that week. There’s about three hundred and something people on that list on the first month so there are 300 people who may have unsubscribed completely or who probably are feeling it’s just a bit too often every few days and they’re busy. It’s good to offer them that choice.

												  collapsed:: true
												  Threat Model - avoid getting sued for torrenting, don't give any website your real location is VPN, don't put address online (eg whois), don't get spammed
												  
												  Most of these are NOT necessarily open source software. 
												  Why? Because they do a good job of making the process to becoming invisible online easy. 
												  
												  For many people this is enough. However for those that want to be completely anonymous and cyber secure, I've created a small guide for you:
												  [Optin]
												  
												  Now continuing on, 
												  A big threat is thieves. Probably there most likely risk for all of you. So what's the solution? Encryption. 
												  
												  If you're being coerced eg by law enforcement or another crazy psychopath then encryption is insufficient. They know you're hiding something so will every effort to get you to reveal it. 
												  
												  The solution? Steganography, AKA

											- What is a taste of success that you can give your reader with your list magnet in order to build credibility and trust?
											- • What is the end result that your reader will benefit from if they read and use the list magnet content?
											- • What are five urgent questions that your site visitor is likely to want answers to right now?
											- • What does your site visitor need to believe or be aware of in order to be interested in your topic and solution?
									- Principles
									  collapsed:: true
										- The perfect Lead Magnet will offer tremendous value within 5 minutes of the opt-in.
										  collapsed:: true
											- This is a “rule of thumb”, of course, but we don’t recommend, for example, a mini-course delivered over 14 days or a 300 page ebook as a Lead Magnet.
											- Your Lead Magnet must be consumed by the prospect for it to have an impact.
										- You simply need to solve a specific problem with a specific solution for a specific segment of your market.
										  collapsed:: true
											- eg How to do X in Y time
									- Upload this to your server or Amazon S3
								- Offer a Tripwire
								  collapsed:: true
								  Affordable, high value e.g. Kindle book
									- Offered to those who have enjoyed the Lead Magnet
									- Affordable e.g. $1-20, though in some markets up to $500 can work
									- Purpose: To change a prospect to a customer
									- Again - can be sold at a loss because it allows you to cultivate a list of buyers.
									- cheap kindle books as tripwire too (like Jeff Walker),

								- Offer a Core Product
								  collapsed:: true
								  Flagship product
									- You can make money on this (likely will make a fair amount due to high value Lead Magnets + Tripwires) but you can delay it by focusing on Profit Maximisers
									- See _Product Evaluation_
									  #ProductEvaluation
								- Offer a Profit Maximizer
								  collapsed:: true
									- Examples
									  collapsed:: true
										- McDonalds makes only $0.18 on $2.09 burger sale (Core Product) but profits an extra $1.14 on fries and Coke upsell (Profit Maximiser)
										- Best Buy sells laptops and plasma TVs (Core Product) with tiny margins but then upsells with warranties, installation and Geek Squad support (Profit Maximiser)
										- Amazon shows you “People that bought this product, also bought that product” to increase the Average Basket Value, also known as maximizing profit.
										- Lots of books like StrengthFinder are just the Tripwire which then results in upsells to coaching, conferences, memberships etc
								- Create the Return Path
								  collapsed:: true
									- Frequent communication offering new Lead Magnets, Tripwires, Core Offers and Profit Maximisers
									- Via Email marketing, content marketing, organic social media, custom audiences and ad retargeting

								- _[Learning Resources_] - Alternative Sales Funnels
								  collapsed:: true
									- Amazon is the best free sales funnel you will find.
									  collapsed:: true
										- He sells the books for $3, as they are a sales funnel for his handwriting analysis courses.
										- The average book on Amazon.com gets 2 reviews.
										- He wrote a book: “63 ways to get Authentic Amazon Reviews” (Amazon).
										- He  used Facebook to get users to opt in via a squeeze page, and then to his Amazon.com book.
										- Gave the book away during the first 48 hours of his launch.
										- $872 spent on Facebook ads.
										- “Sold” 1,600 books in 48 hours.
										- 1,100 were authors. Authors are his target market for his courses on self-publishing.
										- He sells the authors on his $600 course on self publishing by giving away his book.
										- Most great sales funnels I've studied utilize the "Foot in the door technique" while delivering great value. Bart's example proves this:
										- Traffic --> Optin --> Valuable mini offer (Kindle) --> Main offer (Handwriting Course) --> Backend offers (Live events, masterminds, 1on1 coaching, consulting, etc.)
								- _[Learning Resources_] - Product Launch Campaigns
								  collapsed:: true

									- How to Launch the **** out of your ebook
									  Calibre
									- Examples
									  collapsed:: true
										- IBM member example
										  collapsed:: true
											- Update on the bold action I’ve taken this week. Just to recap, I’ve been putting off writing my interview training course for as long as I can remember.
											- So I decided to email my list (c. 2000, although not all are active) and offer them the chance of being part of a special group and test my course at no cost, on the basis that they completed a survey to get into it (test whether they take action once they’ve expressed an interest) and are willing to be a case study for future marketing.
											- Here’s what’s happened so far…
											- Offer email (with short deadline for action) - 'I’m ready to coach you. Are you in?’
											- Email 1 - 29 replied wanting to test the course
											- Email 2 (final call) - 19 replied
											- Email 3 (closing) - 15 replied
											- Total = 63
											- Once they replied they were sent an email to take a short survey about them, the areas they need developing and why they should be considered. That way, I get them to take action and only include those that really want to commit.
											- I also have a 2 reminder emails set-up if they haven’t taken action - deadline for completion is Monday.
											- Total (so far) = 25
											- It’s been fascinating so far. I have a tendency to think through the whole process before taking action - which often leads to inaction. This time around I’ve just gone with ‘what’s the next step I can take’ approach.
											- Now to write the course!! Although I’ve also realised I’ve already got a lot of it written, but not packaged together.
										- https://web.archive.org/web/20160418234137/http://chrisguillebeau.com/product-launch/
										- https://web.archive.org/web/20160418190026/http://nathanbarry.com/app-design-stats/

									- [19:09, 20/4/2016] +44 7497 253028: If so I highly recommend a USB mic
									- [19:09, 20/4/2016] +44 7497 253028: XLR is a hassle because you then have to buy an audio mixer as well
									- [19:10, 20/4/2016] +44 7497 253028: If you're using podcasting mics you have to plug them into a PC, so be aware of that

								- Heil PR40
									- When it comes to podcasting equipment, I don’t know jack when it comes to the technical and geeky stuff behind it. All I know is that I’ve tried a lot of other recommended microphones, and this one by far produces the best sound quality.
									- It is not a USB mircophone – it has a regular 3-prong jack like you’d see on professional microphones that musicians use, so in addition to the microphone you will need a mixer as well, plus the cables to connect the mixer to your computer. It’s a lot of equipment, but if you’re serious about recording some high-quality audio, this is the kind of stuff you need.

								- Libsyn for hosting
								  collapsed:: true

								- SPI Smart Podcast Player
								  https://smartpodcastplayer.com/
								- Audacity
								  collapsed:: true
									- Noise Removal
									  collapsed:: true
										- Noise Removal.
										- Now, with Noise Removal what happens is that, well, when you record a podcast there’s all sorts of residual noise that’s going to get picked up on your microphone. For example, you got air conditioning running in the background, kids playing outside, cars honking, your drunk landlady is screaming after the thing’s scored. Well, Audacity has a nice little Noise Removal tool that allows you to take that noise out and make your podcast sound a lot more professional.
										- Now, you can eliminate a lot of residual background noise by using a high-quality microphone. I have one here that cost me about $250. But if you don’t want to splurge just yet, you can use this on pretty much any microphone.
										- What you want to do is first you need a recording that consists of nothing but the sound that you want to remove, which would be this here, this waveform that’s almost nothing. That’s the background noise I want to get rid of. Now, what you do is you select a subsection of that. Go to Effect, click Noise Removal, and click Get Noise Profile. This is so Audacity knows what you want to get rid of. Now deselect what you selected and that’s very important; you have to deselect it because you want to make sure that the entire podcast gets removed, not just that one little section. Now go back to Noise Removal and click Okay.
										- Now, you can change up these settings if you really want to, but I find the default ones seem to work well, but here we go. As you can see all that background noise is gone now, and if I play back the podcast for you it will sound a lot more professional and a lot nicer.
										- That’s all you really need to know. In fact, you can actually record your podcast using another program, but you can still use Audacity for noise removal. All you need to do is import the MP3 or whatever audio format you’re using, import it into Audacity, select the section that consists of nothing but noise you want to remove, and use the Noise Removal so Audacity will take care of the rest. Hoping this video helps all you guys out, helps you create better quality podcasts.

							- iTunes
								- To do podcasting "right," do 10-20. Hold on tight. Get on Sticher. Release them all the same day. Boom, that's how to game the iTunes algos.
								  [source[ Mike Cernovich (@PlayDangerously) http://ift.tt/1B8exJE
							- Rebrand podcast - audio master class.
							- Short URL for my optin
								- In nearly each session, I mention an easy url that forwards to my landing page here on the blog that describes my eBook, shows a few testimonials and includes an opt-in form.
								- The “easy url” is key because people are not reading the content – they are listening to it. So, you’ll have to rely on either show notes, or a very memorable url that people can plug into their browsers.
								- This is why I picked up ebooksthesmartway.com. It’s much more memorable and easier to read than “smart passive income dot com slash ebooks, dash, the, dash, smart, dash, way”.

							- Pen and Tablet
							- One of the most frequently asked questions I receive is about how I create some of myYouTube videos.
							- In these particular videos I use an electronic blackboard to teach something, but really all you’re seeing on the screen is an image of a “blackboard” I created in photoshop and a recording of myself writing on top of it.
							- To record what you see on your screen, I use Camtasia for Mac (Camtasia Studios for you PC users), although you could use ScreenFlow here as well.
							- To actually write on the screen, you could use your mouse, but it’s very difficult to get any decent penmanship with it. That is why I use a Wacom Bamboo Pen and Tablet.
							- I absolutely LOVE this thing and often find myself using the pen instead of the mouse even when it’s not necessary.
							- If you have photoshop, it also reads the pen pressure so your writing looks like actual handwriting, and if you flip the pen upside down it acts like an eraser too.

						- YouTube Marketing
							- Optimising YouTube Marketing
								- Optimise videos for your keywords
									- Titles
									- Descriptions
									- Tags
								- Upload a transcript of video
								- Add your URL in the description
								- Add the Call-to-Action overlay
								- Add video responses
								- Encourage and increase interaction
							- YOUR GOALS
								- • Get people off Youtube as quickly as possible and onto your page / onto your email list
								- • is NOT to have our video go viral, itʼs to have Video SEO (Search Engine Optimization)
								- How to Get Traffic & Build an Audience on YouTube in 3 STEPS
								- Step #1: Video Optimization
									- Uncover EXACTLY what your audience is searching for!
									- Google Keyword Tool.
									- Step #1 Action Item:
									- Find 20 RELEVANT Keywords (15-100,000 searches/mo)
									- Example: “Royalty Free Music” (90k searches/mo)
								- Step #2: Video Value
									- Create Unique, Relevant Videos for EACH of Your Keywords.
									- Donʼt Know How to Make Videos? Find Someone Who Does.
									- Donʼt Want to Be On Camera? Use Software That Does it For You.
									- Step #2 Action Item:
									- Schedule a “Filming Day” for the next 5-10 Videos
								- Step #3: Aftermath
									- Tell YouTube (and Google) What Your Video is all About
									- 1. Add Your keyword phrase to the
									- a. Title
									- b. Description (add your URL too!)
									- c. Tags
									- 2. Upload a Transcript

							- Pre-Recorded and in Sales Funnel
								- Yes this is absolutely the power of software like infusionsoft. I know a guy who is doing highly targeted webinars on the hour. They are pre-recorded and what list message you get depends on what actions you've take and where you are in the sequence. It doesn't feel sleazy because it's 95% education so you just got a free class or 6. Because the people don't have to schedule, wait, and come back his engagement is very high and he is doing very, very well.
							- Tips
								- You need to be segmenting and tagging people on your list based on interest. If you're running paid traffic to a webinar, then it won't matter, since you'll always get new people on. Move from AWeber to Convertkit, that's what I'd do so you can automate some of this stuff easier
								- You can get away with bi-weekly or even more if you are rotating the content. If you have one that is about dressing to impress, another that is about beating down the fear and approaching someone, etc. you can easily do even weekly. Like Navid said though segmentation is key.
								- (live, as well as recorded powerpoint with you speaking  over it) - start with "this video is for you if.." to resonate with people and dissuade those not qualified. Set up the need during first half of seminar e.g. this is what happens, this is what could happen (the solution)
							- Webinars: Quick, Easy, Profitable
								- A few months ago, I had planned to launch something, but it just didn’t come together in time. Then, another project was also delayed—leaving me with a calendar of more than twelve weeks with no product development or promotion. I’ve always operated AONC on a very low-key, no-pressure sales model (the majority of our readers never buy anything at all, and that’s fine with me) but I also like building and launching things on a somewhat regular basis.
								- That’s when I had the thought: instead of building a huge program, why not find a way to launch something quickly? Every day, people ask me the same questions over and over. Two very common ones are “How can I get started with traveling?” and “How can I work from the road?” Of course, we answer those questions in considerable detail with the Unconventional Guides products, but not everyone wants a whole product.
								- Therefore, I decided to offer two one-hour classes, one of them a “Beginner’s Guide to Travel Hacking” and the other on “Working from the Road.” I made them simple and cheap: just $29 for each one-hour class, with a free recording and resources page delivered afterward.
								- It actually took me a fair amount of work to prepare the materials, since I wanted to make sure they were jam-packed with helpful info instead of me just talking about general ideas. Still, it wasn’t a huge effort compared to the thrill of launching something quickly. We filled up both classes within a few days of launching, and it ended up being a lot of fun.
								- LESSON: If you can easily do something, do it.
							- Webinar Marketing
							  source:: creativeLive
								- 7 Step Webinar Selling Sequence
									- **SUMMARY**
										- Pre-party.
										- Hook (big reveal at the end).
										- Story.
										- Content.
										- Pitch.
										- Q&A.
										- Follow up.
									- The Meet & Greet
										- 0-5 minutes (before the call)
										- Welcome attendees
										- Check audio levels
										- Request social sharing
									- The Welcome (0-5 minutes mark)
										- Intro (Pre-framing**)
											- Who you are
											- What the call is about
										- House-cleaning
										- Agenda
									- Your Story (5-15 minutes mark)
										- Credibility/social proof
										- The struggle/conflict (pain points)
										- The solution (benefits/outcome)
									- The Content (15-45 minutes mark)
										- Market data
										- Case studies (proof)
										- Teach the WHAT!
										- Give actionable items (some How)
									- The Close (45-60 minutes mark)
										- Introduce new problem
										- Introduction the solution (your product)
										- Benefits of product (what's included)
										- Testimonials
										- Guarantee
										- Price breakdown
										- Call to action (send to checkout page)
										- Scarcity
										- Fast-acting bonuses
									- Questions & Answers (60-75 minutes mark)
										- Highlight the benefits of the product
										- Congratulate buyers
									- Final Thoughts & Goodbyes (75 mins mark)
								- Sequences
									- 1) Webinar Registration email -> One-time offer -> Confirmation page
									  As they've been qualified, they want to take action
									- 2) 60m Live Webinar -> Product Offer
									- 3) Thank Attendees in email (and include sales page URL) -> Replay or Encore -> Announce Closing -> Add Autowebinar

										- Hangouts not only up your level of branding by enabling you to host webinars for free, if you do a Hangout on Air, Google+ simultaneously streams it here and on YouTube, records the video, and sends you the recording via email. Upload that Hangout to YouTube, add the link to your blog with a reader-friendly keyword-rich description, and you’ve got a powerful SEO tool that doubles as a branding resource, allowing people to see you in action.

							- Kickstarter Video
								- projects with videos are twice as likely to succeed on Kickstarter, which means it's low hanging fruit to make campaign successful
								- Proposed video structure
									- Why This Project Is Important (Amplify Emotions)
										- Pain of Working a 9-5 (eg interviews of employees, clip of everyone walking in suits walking into work, voiceover of employees complaining about 9-5)
											- Interviews with Target Audience - short clips of people saying how tired they are of their job, how they wish they could find a way to leave their job
										- Desire For Freedom (e.g. bright happy music overlaid, showing travel in exotic countries, adventure sports, attractive people smiling, drinking cocktails by the beach)
									- Creation Story
										- Our Motivation
											- Explicitly highlighting pain + desire points (like a mini motivational speech)
											- We saw billions spending their whole lives slaving away for little reward, people in jobs lack entrepreneurial skills often but still have the creativity - the idea which is the most important part
										- Introducing our Solution
											- Showcase scanner
												- Since we're going for a professional rather than DIY design and it's a relatively big-ticket purchase it would be better to showcase the scanner in a professional environment eg renting a hall or shopfront for a day
												- We should also possibly hire people to stand around and look interested so it looks like we're showcasing our product at an exhibit
												- If we use these then there's the question of westerners vs indonesians, if the scanner is in bandung however we're not gonna be able to get westerners, unless you want to move the scanner to Bali in the near future
											- Benefits Reel
												- Explain via voiceover over videos demonstrating benefits
												- Allow you to create digital assets for money (showcase video game creation, website, virtual reality simulators, microsoft hololens augmented reality)
												- Doesn't require 3D design skills and can be done in a split-second (showcase frustrated programmer, late night/tired designer, clip of someone stepping into scanner and straight away the asset being created, smiling faces of hot scanned girl + scanner operator)
												- Selling 3D printed objects (showcase Sugacube, wood CNC models, 3D printed, even duplicating household items eg mugs/containers etc)
												- Cheapest 3D scanner on market (comparison clip of $100k+ Sugacube setup with DSLRs vs ours - announce pricepoint will be less than 20% - $19k)
									- Call to Action
										- Why We Need Funding
											- we've built the final version of our 3D scanner, now we need your help to get the word out about this fantastic job escape kit
										- The Ask
											- "Click the green button to Back This Project"
									- Rewards
										- Highlight 2-3 Rewards, use Images
										- $1
											- If you can't afford to buy one right now that's alright, every little helps and we appreciate your support
											- You'll get newsletter with updates of ways to make more money by selling assets, by selling 3D printed objects, augmented/virtual reality/3D printing developments etc to kickstart your creativity for when you finally get a scanner
										- $1k
											- You'll get a 3D printer - in the meantime you can buy assets from our site to print if you don't have any design skills and in the future you can upgrade to our split-second asset creation with the 3D scanner
										- $19k
											- Get your very own scanner with all parts, plus free premium membership to our 3D asset marketplace which makes your user + assets featured on the website
									- Conclusion
										- CTA  "Click the green button to Back This Project"
										- If we don't reach funding goal we'll get nothing and everyone will be sad
										- Summarise - this is your escape route, split-second scanning, no skills required, cheapest option available etc

					- Tools
						- Gear
							- Camera
							  collapsed:: true
								- 1080p is a must
								  collapsed:: true
									- If you are serious about video, then recording at 1080p is non-negotiable. Even if you later export it at 720p for file size, bandwidth, and uploading reasons.
									- There are quality differences between shot in 1080p and 720p, even if later uploaded in 480p
								- The difference is really in the optics of your camera’s sensor and using a fast, fixed focal length lens
								- best budget DSLR cameras that I’d recommend you check out:
								  collapsed:: true
									- Canon T4i with 18-55mm: ~$649
									- Nikon D5100 with 18-55mm: ~$600
								- Also, image quality has a ton to do with your lens choice. Instead of using the packaged lens, pick up a good prime lens (which means that it doesn’t zoom). A 50mm like theCanon 50mm f1.4 for ~$340 or the Nikon 50mm f1.4 for ~$335 is a great start.
								- http://www.smartpassiveincome.com/kodak-remote
								- CAMERA
								  collapsed:: true
								  PROBLOGGER PHOTOGRAPHY (composition, lighting)
									- If you want a camera to learn with, and advance your skill level, you will want a camera that has an adjustable exposure control; sometimes called manual exposure controls, sometimes called PSAM.
									- A few compact cameras have this capability, but with many of these cameras, the range of adjustability is limited. The most flexibility will be with a DSLR.
									- A landscape camera is one that has a wide angle lens. Any camera with a wide angle lens whether it be a compact camera or DSLR is preferable for landscapes.
									- A portrait camera - at least what most people consider good portraits - are those photos that have blurred backgrounds. This technique directs the viewer's attention to the subject, especially with a "busy" background.
									- A camera that can do this needs to have the ability to selectively focus - or blur the background. This requires a "fast" lens, a telephoto lens, or a combination of the two.
									- Again, this is most easily done with a DSLR as compact cameras typically have slow, short focal length lenses - both of which limit your ability to blur the background.
									- Why not buy a beginner DSLR, such as a D3100, then spend the remaining money you have on one or two good lenses (a 50mm f/1.8 would be an excellent lens).
									- A camera such as that has an auto mode, which allows you to take decent photos without knowing anything, and also manual controls that will allow you to learn how to shoot in those modes. And you can add lenses for your shooting needs; portraiture, landscapes, and so on.
									- Virtually any DSLR will have this capability, and I only mention the D3100 as it is probably the most popular entry-level DSLR, and it does have a tutorial mode built into the camera that some find useful for learning how to use it.

										  collapsed:: true

									- A shotgun microphone (Rode Video Mic: $150 or Rode VideoMic Pro: $229) - Those attach to your DSLR camera as shown here. They replace your camera’s microphone, and they do a much better job.
									- An external recording device (Zoom H1: $95 or H4n: $250 or Tascam DR-05: $95 orDR-40: $175) – An external recording device can do wonders for your audio, but they also increase your overhead: You need to sync your camera’s audio with the audio captured from your device. That’s why film-makers use clappers.

									- Not only will it bring all of your videos to your site, it will also bring all of the comments from YouTube. From that point going forward – every video you publish on YouTube will magically appear on your site! Every comment made on YouTube will also show up on your site as well! Even if the video gets a comment 2 weeks after you publish the post – in less than an hour that comment will also show up on your website! Go check out Simple Video Press!
							- Self-Hosted
							  collapsed:: true
								- Also, when you use Amazon, it doesn’t compress the videos for playback like You Tube or Vimeo, so you’ll need to make sure you do that yourself.  Most videos straight out of your camera or imovie (especially if they’re in HD) will be extremely large, and thus will playback very, very slowly.  Download Handbreak to cut these files sizes down to 1/10th of the size while still retaining quality.
								  http://handbrake.fr/
								- Hosting
									- Amazon S3
										- It removes video branding. Whenever you use Vimeo or You Tube you get all their annoying logos and stuff all over the video, it’s not clean, elegant, and isn’t about your brand but about theirs.  Hosting your videos on Amazon removes that aspect of it.
										- No commercial licensing issues. I’ve heard nightmares of Vimeo users who’ve been given 24 hours to remove their content for violating their “no commercial video” purposes.  You’d be surprised what they consider commercial. It’s best not to even go there.
										- Cloud Backup. The more content you create, the more important it is to have multiple backups.  This works as both a great way to distribute content, and back it up.

									  http://flowplayer.org/

										- http://www.blogmarketingacademy.com/recommends/evp. While Optimizepress has a built-in video player which is perfectly suitable, one of the (many) capabilities of EVP which make it nice is the fact that it can secure the location of any videos you put into your membership site. Which means tech-savvy people can’t find the location of your video and link to it while bypassing your membership site. EVP does a lot of other nifty things that make it truly the best video player on the market, but for membership sites, the security is pretty handy.
