					- Own the Racecourse-style
					  #OwnTheRacecourse
						- Why
							- Own the platform that you build your online business on. Own the websites that you publish on. This puts you in control and not at the whim of other people and their property.
						- Video syndication via team
							- 20m time yourself, rest distributed to team
							- 1) Gather ideas from customers (especially broad problems)
							- 2) Record video using tripod and remote
							- 3) Edit in screen flow for Mac with watermark on intro and outro then export to Dropbox
							- 4) Then team proposes headlines by email and bullet point teaser benefits (with time stamp), press release for it and get original image for post. Upload to amazon s3, YouTube, embed in Leadplayer for blog, put link on forums, transcript PDF on curated blog, RSS feeds, weekly digest on scribd and pushed to PRweb.
							- 5) Then team emails link to YouTube. Which you can then syndicate to personal twitter, Facebook, tweet, google+, Pinterest, email to channel
							- Use intro + excerpt in email list
						- Tools Checklist
							- You will need the Blubrry plugin to play iTunes podcasts on your website
							- you’ll need a YouTube subscribe plugin to increase the number of subscribers to your YouTube channel.
							- You’ll need to submit your iTunes podcast show once including the appropriate images which I think a 1400 x 1400.
							- You’ll need some kind of digital camera or a laptop or iMac with camera in it, perhaps a tripod, some nice lights, lapel sound and a remote control for the camera.
							- You might need some professional bumpers for intros and outros, both for video and audio.
						- Create Content
							- Listen to http://media.blubrry.com/intranet/p/d2rp9ogikj5dfh.cloudfront.net/podcast/how-to-create-content-that-gets-traffic.mp3
							- Prioritise 6 topics from broadest to narrowest for a 3-5m video
								- e.g. Business > Marketing > Content Marketing > Tools > Wordpress SEO/scribe
							- Look down at notes on phone in between points because we can edit those out
							- Some ideas for topics are: “How to”, remember to embed some cross sells. That’s simply mentioning other products in your chocolate wheel. If you’re in a web news update, mention SEO services. If you’re in an internet marketing news update, mention your SEO services. Just cross promote other things in your brand. That’s just a natural relevant thing that you would say regardless if you’d own it or not and it seems unbiased. Also suggestions, it’s great if you actually use the word suggestion because now you’re becoming authoritative. I suggest you do this, I suggest you do that. They’ve come to you for advice and you’re suggesting solutions.
							- Remember to pitch it as news. I usually open my videos with, “I’m James Schramko, this is …… news.” And I use the channel there. This is SEO news, this is internet marketing news. Now remember we’ve segmented this by channel even in our list so when someone’s on the SEO news, they are getting one SEO video a week. When they’re on the internet marketing news, they’re getting one internet marketing video per week.
						- Production
							- Screenflow editing

							- **In-depth**
								- I import my SD card video Raw MOV file into Screen Flow for editing.
								- I watermark the entire video from start to finish with my URL, just like I have on the bottom of this slide. You can see on the bottom left, it says OwnTheRacecourse.com. That is because I’m going to use snippets of the video in different places and when I syndicate it in one of the future steps; I want people to know where it came from.
								- I also put a little intro and outro, it’s just a little branded logo that shows people a little bit of professionalism. It just steps it up a notch from the backyard operator. But I will give you this tip. Keep it short. As soon as they see the outro that they’ve seen before they switch off.
								- Now you export the edited version as an MOV.
								- I do a little edit. I know some basic Screen Flow editing. How to do a ripple delete, how to cut out ums and ahs, I chop the part where I look to my notes. I’ve seen some people leave their notes in. There’s a guy who does a video series and every few minutes he looks to the left, reads his note and comes back. I don’t know why he doesn’t edit it out and he has a professional editor as well. It looks terribly unprofessional. In my case I just cut that bit so it’s point, point, point, point, point, end.
								- If you want to go for gold, then at the beginning of the video tell people what you’re going to cover in the video and at the end you can do a recap. That’s the best possible standard. I generally don’t do that. Maybe because I like the curiosity and I’m not popping a call to action just after the intro and maybe because I don’t fully know everything I’m going to talk about because I tend to do it in one take.
								- http://www.iskysoft.com/video-editor-windows.html
								- I put my 6 bullets, I read them all one after the other to the camera and then I take that inside and I edit it. This process is very fast. It seems like a lot of work but it’s so quick. I’m filming a 5 minute video in 5 or 6 minutes because occasionally I’d have to stop because there’s an airplane or a truck drives past because I film outside. But generally this is a very fast process.
								- Now what I do is I load this exported movie to Dropbox and I email my team and I say, “I just loaded such and such video to Dropbox. Now what happens is the team takes over. Now I’m telling you this because people want to know how I do it. But if this is you, then just ignore the team part for now and this is still your job.
								- What the team do is they propose a headline and bullets that is both good for SEO and for sales copy.
								- So they’ll watch the video which is, again, it’s only 3 – 5 minutes long. They’ll say, “This is the headline we want to use, these are the bullet points.” They will actually email that to me and then I just email back and say okay or I might just change one of them.
								- Then the team takes the audio from that video and they add an audio start and finish for podcasting. They’ll also do a word for word transcription. Every single word that I say and you can imagine this is great for SEO. They’ll also prepare a press release to announce the news. And they’ll also create an original image that matches the story or the news.
								- If you’ve seen SuperFastBusiness.com you will be impressed by the images.
								- They’re always original and it’s obvious that they were created just for that story. They are not stock pictures; you’ve never seen them before. You’ve never seen them 150 times in other people’s products. These are original.
								- I’ve learnt this lesson because I used stock images when I started and then one day a lady used one of the images that I’ve used for a really negative article. A lady called Rosalind Gardner and she was basically saying bad things about the internet marketing space and she used the same picture that I’ve sort of built my brand around and a lot of my customers emailed me said, “Hey, she took your picture.” But unfortunately it wasn’t my picture. It’s one I had the rights to use but so did she. From then on I had original images. We also watermark the image. This is something I’ve learnt through experience and that’s why it’s easy for you to shortcut to this.
								- As soon as you can afford to get an illustrator, it is ideal. (We will have this service from March 2013)
						- Publish
							- Upload video to youtube
							- Upload MP3 to Amazon S3
							- Add S3 to blog post (blubrry plugin submitted to iTunes)
							- Embed video on blog using LeadPlayer (http://lnx2.co/2fv)
							- Add headline, bullets with time codes, transcription, image
							- team emails links to youtube and posts
							- **Here’s the publishing checklist.**
								- The team will upload the video to YouTube.
								- They’ll be sure to set descriptions, tags, and link back to my website to the post where they’re going to put the YouTube video which they’ve drafted.
								- They’re going to upload the MP3 to Amazon S3 ready for podcasting.
								- They’ll add the S3 link from Amazon S3 to the blog post into the Blubrry plugin which we’ve already submitted to iTunes once. Once it’s done once, we never have to do it again.
								- They’ll then embed the video on the blog using LeadPlayer.
								- I highly recommend this. It doubles the amount of opt-ins that I’m getting to my website. I’ve done feature posts on this. You can easily find my review of it and there are bonuses available if you buy through me but this is a great feature. What it does, it makes your YouTube video a nice clean looking player and it allows you to put your opt-in in the video.
								- You add the headline, the bullets with time codes, the full transcription and the image to the post where you’ve put the video.
								- Then I will embed the video into forums.
								- I will load it up to SuperFastBusiness. I will load the business news to SilverCircle. Most phpBB forums and most Bulletin forums will allow an embed. If you’re lucky, other people will embed them. In fact yesterday I saw one of my customers had embedded one of my videos into the Warrior Forum, which is great. Once you make sharable high value content, people might actually share it. So next time you think of a spinning tool or a splog submitter, think about this. Is it really that good a content that it could be shared naturally because once you reach that threshold that’s when things tip.
						- Syndicate
						  #Syndication https://workflowy.com/#/66255035dbb4
						- MEASURE
							- Google and Twilert alerts
							- sales in cart by product
							- email opens, clicks
							- total subscribers
							- daily subscriber additions
							- YouTube viewers and subscribers
							- Facebook likes
							- facebook CPM CPC
							- feedback from new and leaving customers
							- analytics
							- adwords
							- google webmaster tools
							- identify bottlenecks
							- This is the measuring checklist.
								- Check your Google and Twilert alerts.
									- It will show you the response to your press releases and your blog posts. I am getting alerts instantly with my video thumbnails, with my blog posts, with my syndicated blog posts, with my press releases. We post content, it comes back that day. It’s amazing how fast Google is to pick that content up. They must be literally all over the site.
								- Sales in the cart by product.
									- Why is our website business doubled in the last 30 days? It’s because we got the customer feedback. We promoted it to our perfect customer audience. Cross promoted it to the other people in that audience especially with the weekly news summary. That is a great way to cross promote your products because you’re putting the news from all the channels even though someone may have come from one channel. And then the sales go up in the cart. That’s exactly what I’ll measure.
								- Emails, opens and clicks.
									- You got to find out if people are fading on you or are becoming unresponsive, or if they continue to open emails and you can check things like complaint rates and click-throughs. You can also use your tracking links, your Google analytics tracking links and check out what’s actually happening there with trends and graphs and charts.
								- Total subscribers is something we definitely watch.
									- In this case, I’m collecting let’s say a dozen emails a day from my main news blog. At the end of the month, I’ve sent 70,000 emails. If I still have more subscribers than I start with at the month, then my business is growing. There will be some people who unsubscribe and leave, there’ll be some people who come. But I’m pretty certain if they’ve got between 4 and 20 emails from me that month and they’re still on my customer list, they’re a red hot prospect, if not a buyer already.
								- Daily subscriber additions, that’s something that we look at.
									- How many people are coming on board. So we’re able to pick up a big increase by adjusting our LeadPlayer videos. You can also change the layout of the website to encourage page views or conversions on particular products. It’s important that you measure everything.
								- YouTube viewers and subscribers.
									- I’m definitely watching views and subscriptions. I want to know which videos they watch. How many subscribers? Adding the little WordPress YouTube subscribe widget had definitely increased the number of subscribers and I think over time again that will snowball.
								- Facebook likes.
									- I just went past 4,000 Facebook likes. It wasn’t that long ago I had about 1,600 so I put a bit of effort on it to build it up and that again it’s a bigger snowball. Literally reaching twice as many people with every post because there’s more people there.
									- Have a look at your Facebook cost-per-thousand or cost-per-click metrics for your paid campaigns. Generally it will start a little higher and it will get down to a lower amount. It can be quite a low amount. If Facebook likes your ad, if you get great click-throughs, if your content is always fresh.
									- Now if you speak to a Facebook expert and there are plenty of those around us. They will tell you it’s really image driven. Put fresh great images and remember we talked about having brand new, fresh, original images. Well that’s perfect for Facebook. Your campaign is going to stick because your fresh images continue to provide something of interest. I’ve seen stock images that I’ve seen over and over again and I’m just blind to them.
								- Measure feedback from new and leaving customers.
									- You should definitely ask people who arrive into your system why they bought and you should ask people who leave, why did they leave. And that way you’ll find out what’s wrong. What you can fix.
								- Pay attention to your analytics and your goal tracking and your funnel.
									- Have a look at your Adwords. See your banner click-throughs and conversions.
								- Look at Google Webmaster Tools.
									- You’ll see a huge impact in the number of pages indexed on your site; the queries, the click-throughs, the search positions.
								- Identify bottlenecks.
									- Always ask yourself, “What is our biggest sticking point? What’s slowing our content down? Am I not getting new ideas? Do I need to build a bigger team? Are my ads wrong? What’s the bottleneck that you can fix? These are things you want to measure.
						- Refine
							- Using the data gathered optimize future content creation, production and syndication.
							- Refine
								- fine tune notes in Evernote
								- adjust templates for screenflow editing
								- change design layout
								- adjust messaging
								- add more targeted content
								- bring on more syndication points
								- adjust the mix of sales offers
							- The point here is we need to refine.
								- Fine tune your notes in Evernote. When I look at my likes and dislike metrics from YouTube views, I go and make a note in Evernote. When I get metrics from Analytics that show me search queries are getting good exposure, for example, I started ranking well for the phrase Wistia, so I just make a note in Evernote. Maybe I’ll do a post where the topic is something about Wistia and I’ll put that in the page title and that will just reinforce an already good ranking. Now if I have 10 separate pages, ranking for Wistia, Google will start to think my siteis quite an authority on that phrase and it will lend me some favour.
								- Adjust your templates for ScreenFlow editing.
								- I open up a template for ScreenFlow so when I want to record, it’s very easy. I just drop in my RAW file, the intro and outro is already there. All I have to do is adjust the colour, check the volume, cut out my edit points and render. It is a 15 minute process.
								- Change the design layout.
								- You will get info from your heatmap and opt-ins. Just keep tweaking it. Change the colours, keep moving things around but test it. My site has probably had 50 changes since I’ve started. But whatever SuperFastBusiness is today, you can be certain that that’s after a lot of tests and it’s probably converting quite well and it probably can convert even better when I get tests results. It will be continually refining situation.
								- Adjust your messaging.
								- I know now that people do not like certain things. I will just eliminate that from my conversation. I’ll give you one example of that. They’re not that interested in things that are coming. They’re very interested in things that are here now. That’s the kind of society we live in I guess. Show me what’s here now. Don’t tell me about what’s coming, just show me what you’ve got now.
								- Add more targeted content. Just keep tuning into your audience.
								- Bring in more syndication points.
								- Find out who else could syndicate your newsletter too. What other events could you go to to pick up some people to syndicate to? Which other forums can you participate in? Who else can you send YouTube link to that they might share it? Whichever Facebook group’s perfect for you?
								- Adjust the mix of sales offers.
								- Most of my videos will refer to some other part of my business. I don’t often promote things outside my business except when I find something exceptional like LeadPlayer then I really do promote because it’s made such a difference to my business, I want to share it with my customers and they thank me for it.
								- They’re very excited when I get excited about something because I’m a pretty tough judge. I reject about 3 or 4 offers a day to promote things or “I’m just reaching out to you James, blah blah blah.” Yeah I’m busy.
								- I’m building and creating amazing products and services of my own and I’m talking to my customers about them and solving their problems. That’s my job. So keep adjusting the mix of sales offers that you have to get that nice balance.
								- If you’re going to publish news, make sure you put news in there.
								- If you’re going to have tips, put tips in there. It shouldn’t be a straight sales pitch every time. If you have a long term customer, they will put up with a little bit of pitching before they turn you off. A good example there is Drayton Bird who sends out mostly good emails but when he has an event coming, he pitches like 3 times a day and I finally moved him to my check folder which means he’s one stage from being booted. It’s just that he sends good content about once or twice a week that saves him. So that’s what I suggest.
								- Make it so that your customer can’t really unsubscribe because they really want to receive your information. And that’s the big shift from traditional internet marketing that people are usually just sending crap.
						- _More_
							- https://web.archive.org/web/20160423032624/http://www.superfastbusiness.com/business/owntheracecourse-reloaded/
							- https://web.archive.org/web/20160423032640/http://www.superfastbusiness.com/web/own-the-racecourse-conversion-update/
					- Tips
						- Recording+Editing
							- Close Enough to See the Whites of Our Eyes
								- A captivating video includes close-ups. Close enough to see the whites of the eyes of the “actor”. This is what transfers emotions effectively and is an amazing way to show emphasis.
								- Close-ups are easy with two cameras - one close, one further away
								- One Camera Close-Ups
									- One way is to edit in your close-ups. Some video editing programs like Final Cut Pro X allow you the option to “crop” a video. If you do this though, you will actually lose some of the quality of your shot. However, if your video was shot with good lighting and 1080p quality, then the drop in quality shouldn’t be obvious.
										- Crop - e.g. sometimes appear on left, sometimes on right
										- Slow zooms
									- Shoot the video twice (though lip syncing may be off)
						- Recording
						- Editing
							- Cutting to remove filler words
							  Umm, ahh, hmm. It is natural to pause and think about what to say. What does not come out well though is to leave even 0.5 sec of those moments in your video. You must learn the art of cutting!
							  
							  As you see, there is minimal cutting in my first video, while my second video is much more sophisticated.
							  
							  Now you might be worried that if you cut your shot in scene #1 and scene #2, then when you combine the two scenes together you will notice a “jump” in the video.
							  
							  You’re are right. You will notice a slight jump that may or may not be bothering.
							  
							  If you want to get rid of it, then change the angle of scene #2.
							  
							  For example, scene #2 may be zoomed in. Or, scene #2 may be taken by the footage of the second camera that was shooting from a slightly different angle.
							  
							  If you do this your final video will look natural. No jumps. Just a seamless progression.
						- transitions, effects, background music, branding intros, calls to action

						- Ideas
							- Edit YouTube videos. Make my YouTube header look good. Add logos onto my videos. Create a playlist for my channel. Create a trailer for my channel. Appear in YouTube videos by demonstrating alpha posture exercises, meditation, and other topics I want to teach. Follow me around all day and record stuff we do. We could do some interesting “day in the life” videos. Film me juicing veggies and cooking stuff in a crock pot. We can then upload and edit that into YouTube. Create some artistic videos like this:

							- https://www.creativelive.com/courses/youtube-marketing-simple-video-strategies-grow-your-business-james-wedmore#class-materials

						  http://realstyle.vtacademy.hop.clickbank.net/
						- iPhone Video Hero - Created by Jules, a 20+ year TV and film veteran (he’s worked with guys like the BBC!), this course shows you exactly what you can do with your iPhone to create incredibly hot looking videos to help promote your business and brand.
						- GideonShalwick.com - As mentioned above, my online video go-to-guy. I’ve known Gideon for several years and he’s one of the nicest, most genuine people you’ll ever meet. He’s also got some serious marketing chops, on top of his video geek side!
				- Resource Repository
				  Purpose: Get brand awareness by providing high-value free stuff
					- Offer a free resource repository e.g. images
					  Paint Gallery store - So let say you sell paintings (the ones that cost $20 which you hang up on the wall). You can go after different interior designers that focus on office furniture, or home decor and write a reverse guestpost about them on your website. Then let them know about it.
					  
					  Reverse - Everyone's always crying about copyrighting photos right. Well, how about you turn the game on it's head. Create a huge repository of photos, images, and pictures you have for sale in your gallery store, and watermark them all in a bottom corner. Nothing retarded, cause this won't work if it's a retarded watermark across the whole image - fucking it up. Then reach out to 100 bloggers who update their blog more than once a week, and let them know they can use your photos in your repository for free as long as they keep the watermark in place. If they would like to put a link to the piece of work so their visitors can buy the piece and hang it, that'd be cool of them and their visitors can appreciate it. Leave it at that.
					  
					  There are going to be some that will crop out the image and remove your watermark, don't waste time or worry about those. The vast majority of them though will appreciate a free repository, and would most likely link back. Even if they don't directly link back, the images have your watermark on them, driving direct traffic. Let say you do this outreach to 100 bloggers a week for 10 weeks straight, that's 1,000 bloggers, and 30% use your image with about 20% of them create links that's 300 bloggers distributing your brand and 200 creating direct links to your website for direct sales - increasing your exposure weekly - since they blog more than once a week.  [Icon7]
					  
					  If you get really clever, you can create an affiliate program and tell them that they can get a commission whenever one of their visitors buys a painting - so it's a win-win for them, and a major win for you. They get free images and can make some extra cash, while you have a growing network of bloggers blogging everyday while spreading your brand and links indirectly around the internet. But, but, but, too many anchors, bla, bla, bla - you didn't tell them what type of links to create, so they'll most likely be custom according to the blogger's desire, damn Google's got you brainwashed...
					  
					  You see the lies Matt Cluts told you? Not to link or get a link from any website? Links are what drive traffic? If you have no links from traffic, you are then reliant on one source. In my scenario, I've got 300 bloggers creating links, and brand awareness for me. In your scenario, your using Search Engine Pray to pray and hope search engines send you traffic... one day...
					  
					  If people are already jacking your photos, you can use Google Image search to find them, and let them know that you have a free big repository of images; you ask that you keep the watermark, a link, or even a link to your affiliate program so they can make money. You go from being a dick cause they stole your images, into making them your partner/allies while generating both of you revenue. 
					  
					  
					   Forums are usually where most gifs are reposted, so that's going to be the main game plan. Bribe a couple of high profile posters and give them access to the repository and the ability to upload their own gifs, not necessarily having to be sloth gifs. They'll use that, and then you can open up the image sharing site to the masses once it becomes popular.
					- Watermarking
					   Watermarking - on photos without dropping a backlink - Increases brand awareness and direct traffic (also type in search traffic). Think Suddenly_Ass's photos, you know there are several with watermarks that we all type in to get more of that shit, let's be real.
					  
					  Type in traffic works best if you have a short or simple URL - HowToMakeMoneyInTheTwentyFirstCentury.com is not going to work, too many chances of a typo (If you must get a shorter URL and 301 redirect it then do it - remember fuck that Google shit). Then there are some that we'll use Google Image search to find more from the collection. Watermarks drive in traffic - shit has been working before there was a Google.
					  
					  Don't be obnoxious with your watermark, sometimes the more subtle the better. It's like you have to be cool about it. That way people like Suddenly_Ass are more likely to post it around the internet and spread it for you. ::emp:: has a great guide on taking your images to the next level: https://web.archive.org/web/20130727080616/http://deepercontent.com/hands-on/enhance-how-to-turn-images-into-killer-traffic-magnets 
					  
					  Seriously think about it for a moment, what niche/subject matter doesn't have photos or imagery at some level? You can watermark everything, videos, PDFs, white papers, put your domain url/brand everywhere. Don't have photos, find some, then watermark that shit for days. We did this for an online entertainment magazine property back in the day - 2003. We went to forums that fit our demo - guys looking at sexy chicks, and just dropped image albums through the forum. It was a common thing to do, so no one thought twice. People took those photos, re-posted them, and till this day we are still getting traffic from those photos.
					  
					  If you can get people to spread the content for you, that's a lot easier. A little more difficult to do with videos, and content, well that's pretty fucking boring, but photos - people will re-post them, re-blog them, share them, save them to their hard-drives, and so on.

						- Content Curation
							- i.e. gathering relevant content
							- Why
								- Too much sales and self-promotion on social media is annoying
								- Balance it out with posting Education + News resources in order to be relevant and useful to your audience
							- How
								- ((644c0b2c-971e-4f9b-aca2-3ccc41397f16))
									- Manage them with https://feedly.com
								- Twitter follow interesting or influential people/organisations
								- Create a list of websites, magazines, newsletters etc to follow
						- Content Creation
							- Types of Content
								- Educational
									- It stays high importance, value, accuracy and relevance even years down the line
								- News
									- It becomes of low importance, value, accuracy or relevance years down the line
									- Timely news examples
										- "Prostate laser therapy recommended to NHS - BBC News"
										- "Rise in type 2 diabetes in children 'deeply worrying' - BBC News"
								- Own Sales
									- Advertising for our own product or service with a Call to Action
								- Affiliate Sales
									- Advertising for other's products or services with a Call to Action
							- By Platform
							  See "By Platform" bullet
						- General Strategy
							- Ratio of Content Types
								- Around 50% of all Tweets should be Educational
								- 30% Sales
								- 10% News
								- 10% mixed/other
							- Review analytics regularly
								- analytics.twitter.com
								- See social media automation tool analytics
								- _Related: _Conversion Optimisation - Analytics Tools
								  #ConversionRateOptimisation https://workflowy.com/#/18d9367caba4
						- Platforms
							- Twitter
								- Strategy
									- Favorite All Mentions
										- Anyone who mentions a post of ours will receive a favorite.
										- We think it is poor form to retweet every single mention of your blog since it looks too promotional. We believe twitter should be run as a micro-blog so the main takeaway should be *content*. It should give viewers a sneak peek into what the blog is about.
										- If you automate the process of favoriting every tweet, you’re going to end up favoriting a lot of negative tweets as well. This will come off as “passive aggressive”. Who cares. These people lose their cool and call even *more attention* to your blog! Let them flip out since passive aggressive behavior is for two year olds and women. We strongly suggest automating your “favoriting” process because it helps you grow your pla
									- New Tweet Creation
										- Principles
											- Don't use hashtags in the middle of a sentence, they go at the end
											- Before choosing a hashtag, search for it on Twitter to see if it gets tweeted multiple times a day
											- Tweets with pics get more engagement. If you use a URL, often it auto-expands to show an image also
										- If it's a reusable tweet (e.g. it's Educational type) then add the tweet to the Google sheet as well

							- Facebook
								- Join similar groups and assist
								- 1. Friend thought leaders in your industry. Although a large percentage of the people will not accept your friend request, at least 10% of the thought leaders should.
								- 2. Start engaging with your audience by posting stuff on your wall. The stuff shouldn't be about your company, but more so about industry news and events related to your sector.
								- 3. To create the most engagement post things that are short and during times you think your "target friends" are online.
								- 4. Start commenting on your friends' wall and like messages that you like within your feed.
								- 5. Start asking questions on your wall. These questions should be related to your industry. By asking questions you'll start to see more engagement from your "target friends".
								- 7. At this point you should be posting information about your company 30% of the time. But, again, don't hard sell your product... only do soft pitches.

								- Use and build Communities
								- Use Ripples and find who can spread my posts to the largest audience (add to circles and engage)
								- Make posts using hash tags and +tag people/brands
									- Google+ posts that receive the most comments are resource-type articles with a lot of links. Those covering Google+ how-to information are heavily shared, along with any tutorials. Write a killer post and link back to your own blog for additional resources. Use hashtags with relevant topics so it’s easily found, and you’ve created an outstanding resource that will get you noticed, and will increase traffic to your blog.
									- Add value to make the social network better e.g. forum tutorial, FAQ, help others improve their posts, Best Of, every tip/trick/tool of the platform
									- Headline under 60 char so it's on one line pref
									- Compelling opening http://thecopybot.com/2011/08/sedaris-first-sentence/ http://www.copyblogger.com/seductive-hook/
										- Review good opening lines before every first sentence  http://www.copyblogger.com/first-sentence/ http://www.shortlist.com/entertainment/films/50-best-opening-lines-in-movies http://www.stylist.co.uk/life/the-best-100-opening-lines-from-books http://www.complex.com/music/2013/02/the-100-best-opening-lines-in-rap-part-1-100-51/
								- Test new ideas on Google+ — then re-create on your own blog

						- If they try to claim content as their own
							- This is the angle I'm working on right now--simply showing him the proof, and asking that either 1) my name/bio/backlink be restored or 2) the article be removed. Waiting to hear back. If he lets this go more than... say... 2 weeks, I'll first send a cease and desist letter. If THAT doesn't work, I'll consider the DMCA letter.
					- Media Outreach
					  **Short-term: **Promote other promotional marketing with my own website or content marketing platform.
					  **Long-term: **Make sure to leverage social proof on own site
						- Establish Credibility
							- readership, social proof (featured by which sites/people) or 3rd party research/data
						- Getting Media or Blogger Links (Drafting Technique)
						  #DraftingTechnique
							- Overview
								- Step 1: Make a list of 5-15 competitors. #FindingInfluencers https://workflowy.com/#/e55e04295aa2
								- Step 2: Find where they have been mentioned in the media. If they haven’t been mentioned, find competitors or related companies who have.
								- Step 3: Figure out how to contact the journalists who featured these companies.
									- https://app.asana.com/0/13939091628542/29788829124071/f
								- 4) Drafting Technique Email
								  Hi [NAME], 
								  
								  I was checking out some of your articles on [THEIR WEBSITE NAME] and found this page: [THEIR ARTICLE URL]
								  
								  I noticed that you linked to one of my favourite articles: [COMPETITORS ARTICLE URL]
								  
								  Brilliant!
								  
								  I actually set out to make something similar: [MY WEBPAGE URL]
								  
								  It's like [COMPETITORS ARTICLE NAME], but way more [SPECIFIC USP]. 
								  
								  Would you consider adding a link to it from your page if you think it meets your standards?
								  
								  Either way, I'll see you around at [THEIR WEBSITE NAME]!
								  
								  Cheers!
								  [MY NAME]
								  
								  --------------
								  
								  
								  Hey ____!
								  I noticed that you guys published syndicated content from time to time, which is really helpful since I would have never discovered [link to a good article] if it wasn’t for you, so thanks!
								  I wanted to ask, since I believe we’ve also published a few posts that are a perfect fit for your audience, if you’re interested in potentially re-publishing some works from our site? We have quite a few pieces that touch on topics that regularly do well with your readers, most of which have a fresh perspective that I think they’ll enjoy.
								  Here are a few examples that I think would be well recieved:
								  
								      Article 1
								      Article 2
								      Article 3
								  
								  Let me know if those are a fit and if you think this partnership might be a win-win for us, and if not, no sweat!
								  Have a great week,
								  [Your name]
								- Step 5: Get the competitors of these journalists to also feature (or feature you instead)
							- “The Drafting Technique.”
								- Step 1: Figure out what you’d like to promote.
								- Step 2: Find people who are interested in covering you.
								- e.g. watch where your competitors get guest posts or media writeups
								- + Google search for who has wrote about topic (confirms their opinion) and those who it disconfirms it for)
								- Step 3: Persuade those people to cover you.

					- Worldwide Instant Replying
						- You’ll need your Google alerts and Twilerts setup for your keywords and for your name.
							- Google Alerts sent “As they happen” not “Once a day” or “Once a week”.
							- If you're not getting at least 100 alerts per day you will need to add some more products or find a new niche.
						- Assist keyword searchers
							- Assist on Twitter - search 'keyword' on twitter and share post with them
							- Assist through Google - search 'keyword' and related keywords
								- Use google alerts too and " "
						- Commenting on Twitter Stream

						- Reddit
						- Live Google Updates
							- Go to Google and search for your keyword. If your keyword is a phrase, put it in quotation marks.
								- [product name] [your niche] [competitor products]
								- You have to be creative with your searches to avoid running into all of the other marketers. Especially in the weight loss niche since everyone is just copying the same idea (not a good strategy). Anyway, try things like “lose back fat” or “get rid of a double chin” or even “flabby arms”. Use your brain. Write down tons of keywords you think people in the niche would search for. When you get a good list you should insert those keywords into google keyword planner or similar software to generate even more search terms.
							- Click on “Search Tools” under the search box.
							- Change “Any Time” to “Past 24 Hours”
							- Change “Sorted by relevance” to “Sorted by date” to get the most recent results first.
							- If you want to search Facebook posts, or any other site, you can also do this on Google by entering the following and changing the time and sort as above:
							- Searching for: “your keyword” site:facebook.com
						- BuzzBundle
							- BuzzBundle is a handy piece of software that helps to push more traffic to your review website by engaging with your target audience through popular social media sites (Facebook, Twitter, LinkedIn, Google+), forums, Q&S sites, videos and blogs. It automatically scrapes the web to find the terms you are targeting (you can use the same ones you are targeting via Google search) and replies, directing everyone back to your review site for more information. When set up correctly, this can produce conversions on autopilot, even while you are sleeping.
							- You can get a free BuzzBundle trial in order test out the software. Once you have your trial registered, check out our BuzzBundle Tutorial that explains how to properly use the software and how we used it to generate 594k page views in a day! http://www.rosshudgens.com/
						- Real-Time Reactive Promotion
							- Monitor who is saying what about my competition on these social websites. When I hear people complain I hit them up and tell them how my company can solve their problems
							- Capitalise on competitor crises
						- Point of view
							- 3rd Party: Whenever something about one of your big competitors gets alerted to you, drop your brand/link and state, "yeah this is great, BUT has anyone tried YOUR BRAND, it's suppose to be a better alternative, does anyone have experience with it?" - Traffic Leaks. Switch up the style and flow. To make sure you aren't posting the same thing over and over, try coming up with 20 new catch phrases/intros once a week as practice.
							- 1st Person: The direct route is you come in as a representative for your brand to comment accordingly - tell the brand they did a good job and tell them as a competitor we plan on doing a better one.. You can also act as if you were to confront your competition in a public debate like a politician in one of those town-hall debates. Have your rebuttals written out, and catch phrases ready. The attack can be in the style of a question and answer in comparison to your brand.
							- If you have a team asking questions and commenting that looks impartial and sometimes asks you the hard questions as well and you answer them with enthusiasm, while the other party doesn't even answer, it only makes your brand look more powerful.
						- Real-Time Affiliate Marketing
							- https://app.asana.com/0/search/38545321942262/29628947923012
					- Assist on Q&A sites
						- Search 'keyword' > there may be subtopics within site too
						- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Comparison_of_Q%26A_sites e.g. yahoo! answers and quora
						- Q&A automation - extracting from one then using on another https://workflowy.com/#/f7af024bfa17
						- Own Q&A site in your niche OR local language
						   Then as the site grows, "advertise" your money site in the ad spots. Take relevant questions from other Answers platforms, throw the answers through WordAi, and get things started yourself. Then comment bomb other answers platforms, Facebook, Twitter, and others? How about having a bot that sits idly by on twitter, and whenever someone mentions a topic in your thread, you interact with them and let them know the answer may be on your Q&A site
						- QUORA
							- Answer newest questions - http://www.quora.com/open_questions
							- Answer questions by topic http://www.quora.com/Aaron-Sollesse
					- Forums
						- Use signature
						- "I want to give back to the community" thread
						  Once you get familiar with the climate of the forum, drop a "I want to give back to the community" thread, and talk about the different types of insurance products available, and how members can ask the right questions to their agents. DO NOT self-promote all in up the thread - no one really gives a shit about a nobody's website just yet but they will take free advice, should be obvious, but people don't get it. At the end simply state if anyone is interested in learning more, just drop me a question in this thread OR PM me for something specific.
						- Fake Negative Posts
						      Sometimes an indirect method to getting traffic may work. Getting traffic may require you to point out something on your own site, obviously not admitting you own the site, and saying some random shit like "Hey, is XYZ's login down for anyone else?", Or "Does anyone know how to find XYZ's faq page" - Xrumer style of spam  [Big Grin]
						  
						      Several months ago some random forum in my niche started talking about a top 10 list I had, since someone in their niche started blasting that their company was #1 on Facebook. The forum thread stated the content was drivel, and paid for by the company, etc. Un-known to them, they increased my SEO since they were all linking to my content and I've had a nice healthy boost to my SERPS for that term cause I was getting links form and talked about, even negatively, from an authority in the industry. Thanks.

		- Build Relationships with Influencers (Direct Competitors)
		  id:: 644c0a78-5112-425c-a6d7-1bf7b65c78ae
		  #Influencers
			- Process
				- 0) Finding Influencers
				  collapsed:: true
				  #Influencers-Finding
					- Intro
					  collapsed:: true
						- Search relevant keywords. You’ll find all of the top bloggers in your niche if you search the keywords that you want to rank for.
						  collapsed:: true
							- See who your favorite bloggers interview. Bloggers interview the people they admire.
							- Find top lists. If you’re able to find a few of the top ranking, top blogger lists, there’s a good chance that the top bloggers are actually featured.
						- Find you deepest and most dedicated true believers. Share all of these steps and ask who will stand for the cause. Bring them into the fold and empower them to lead with knowledge, support, tools and access. If you do this right, often your torchbearers will appear out of the woodwork, without you even having to seek them out. That’s ideal.
						- How LYL is doing it: We provide all kinds of tools in our Passionate Work Toolkit and Live Event Field Kit to give our community all they need to be their own leader. All our courses have the option for deeper involvement. All of our paid team members have come from Living Legends reaching out, asking to help and doing such an amazing job that we decide to officially bring them on. In fact, I won’t hire someone any other way. It must start with a mutual dedication to the cause and revolution.
					- Software
					  collapsed:: true
						- Alltop
						- Using BuzzSumo to Find Influencers Who'll Promote Me
						  collapsed:: true
						  https://app.buzzsumo.com/
							- Search for keyword to find related influencers
							- Sort by Total Shares > View Shares > sort by Reply Ratio
							- Put everyone whose reply ratio is 30% of higher in a spreadsheet (name and ID)
							- Also all the URLs of top content shared
						- QuickSprout
						  collapsed:: true
							- Enter in URLs > Social Media Analysus > copy all of their top shared blog post URLs
						- Topsy
						  collapsed:: true
							- Copy URLs from QuickSprout into here > select Influencers
							- English and Influential only
						- Bit.ly
						  collapsed:: true
							- Copy URL into bit.ly to see stats and see who drove the most clicks to the URL
							- Find out where they shared it
						- Backlink analysis
						  #SEO https://workflowy.com/#/657c456bdae2
					- Potential Influencer Targets
					  collapsed:: true
						- Internet Marketers
						- Copywriters
						- Youtube Marketrs
						- Find people who have the audience you want… and convince them to send some of their people your way.
					- Other content authority marketers
				- 1) Get Their Respect
				  collapsed:: true
				  #Influencers-GiveValue and demonstrate status to improve likelihood of positive response to first email.
					- A) Demonstrate You're A Fan
					  collapsed:: true
						- Praise
						  collapsed:: true
							- Give Specific Praise
							  collapsed:: true
								- e.g. I only read x number of blogs, best out of x many books, etc
								- Or talk about an entertaining story, style/personality or a deceptively simple tactic they offered
							- Medium
							  collapsed:: true
								- Non-promotional eg comment or email
								  collapsed:: true
									- Tip: Get RSS with push to mobile so early to comment.
								- Promotional (see Proactive Promotion below) https://workflowy.com/#/d64551e3ee45
						- Proactive Promotion
						  collapsed:: true
							- On Social Media
							  collapsed:: true
								- Check influencer's timelines at least 1x/day
								- Use a 120-character tweet so others have space for "RT @AaronSollesse"
								- FollowFriday
								- Recommend them my followers in replies
								- Volunteer to “vote” for any posts that they’re pushing on social media sites like Digg, Del.icio.us and StumbleUpon
								- https://app.asana.com/0/14081627226321/30142839178466
							- Guest Posts / Blog Posts
							  collapsed:: true
								- Then followup email - "Thanks for the great conversation the other day - I learned so much / was so impressed with what you're doing that I did a writeup about you on my blog. I thought you'd like to know - and I look forward to talking to you again. Let me know if you need anything in terms of spreading the word about what you do." Include the link.
						- Show You're A Customer?
						  collapsed:: true
							- Any Product
							  collapsed:: true
								- Once you buy, tweet/blog it and thank them for their product/tell them you'll review it soon/ask questions
								- “I bought your product and I finished it and I got XYZ results,”
								- Then, offer to write a testimonial/case study/product review.
								  collapsed:: true
									- <Product Name> Review, include good/bad/ugly
									- Thank her for her product + Let the creator know you have a review up and are letting people know about your experiences + Ask if there's anything you can do for her.
									- Do a few follow-up reviews, and repeat the last two steps. Leave your first review open-ended. Maybe there's more you can add onto it that you didn't cover in your first review. Maybe you're just giving a progress update. But follow up. Give more exposure.
									- Extremely effective method for networking + targetted traffic, especially useful to let beginners know if a product is good from an experts POV
								- Case Study Technique
								  collapsed:: true
									- Email them saying their advice worked
									  collapsed:: true
										- This works best for their new products so they'll feature you
									- e.g. Leo Babauta could say I read Zen To Done - write up comprehensive review
									- collapsed:: true
									  1. Choose the influencer
										- Networking - Asana
										  https://app.asana.com/0/13448633525131/15089236335451
									- Buy their product / use their free advice
									  There are few rules of thumb to keep in mind here.
									  
									      The more expensive the product, the more an influencer needs good success stories to sell it.
									      The more money you spend on a product, the more you show commitment and stand out.
									      The bigger the influence someone has, the more money you should spend on a product in order to really get their attention.
									  
									  
									  The easy route is to buy their book. But everyone has probably bought their book. This might work OK for mid-tier influencers.
									  For "A-list" influencers you have to get more serious. Do they have consulting services? Do they have a coaching program? Or at the very least, do they have a higher-priced training? Buy that.
									- Show up on their radar
									  collapsed:: true
									  If this is the case, become one of the most valued members of the community.
									  Influencers depend on active, helpful members to set the tone for a group. Be that person. I promise, they will take notice.
									  We know the names of every person that participates regularly in our private Facebook groups. Do this, and you'll make huge blips on their radar.
										- Let them know you bought their product
										  collapsed:: true
										  Relevant info or promotional backup - "im on chapter 5 and its great!"
											- Let them know you're already getting a lot out of it (blog post, comment on theirs or tweet them) thank them /tell them you'll review it soon/ask questions
											- Mention when you learn something that really strikes you.
											- Share Specific Results/Plan of Action i.e. I learned [x] from them; now I can [x] etc or how you will be taking action
											  “I bought your product and I finished it and I got XYZ results,”
											- Ask product-based questions that can be answered in 1 tweet
										- Private/paid online communities - be incredibly helpful in them
										- Webinars
										      Take notes during so that an insightful question can be asked during
										      Follow up - thank you email for answering your question + how you’re going to implement that piece of advice. 
										      Follow up case study - share your results.
									- Take action and get results
									  collapsed:: true
										- Take action until you get the desired result. Measure and document your progress.
										- Take screenshots along the way. Keep proof of your success. You'll need it when it comes time to make your case study video.
									- Create a video testimonial/product review
									  collapsed:: true
										- Here's what to include in your testimonial video.
										  collapsed:: true
											- State your name, where you are from and your web site.
											- If you are comfortable with it, quickly share something else about yourself. This makes your video more personal and believable. This should be something that relates to the product and topic.Examples include:* I'm a student at Berklee School of Music
											- * I'm a mother of two children
											- * I've been a software engineer for ten years.
											- Mention which product you bought and used
											- Share what you did and the specific results
											- Include proof of the results if possible
										- If you need help recording your video, see episode 225 where we share how we create our videos. http://www.internetbusinessmastery.com/ibm-225-sell-first-product-pt-2-creating-product/
										- Save the video file in the highest resolution possible. Put it in DropBox or something similar where you can easily share it.
										  collapsed:: true
											- Then, offer to write a testimonial/case study/product review.
											  collapsed:: true
												- <Product Name> Review, include good/bad/ugly
									- Contact the influencer
									  collapsed:: true
										- Find direct contact info for the influencer if possible. If not, then sending a message to their staff or office will also work. You can also do a first contact through social media.
										- Your first email should simply ask for permission to send them the video. Here is a template.
										  Subject: How I used NAME OF PRODUCT to get SPECIFIC RESULT
										  Hi NAME,
										  I'm an owner/buyer/student of NAME OF PRODUCT.
										  Can I send you a video I made that shows how I used PRODUCT NAME to get SPECIFIC RESULT?
										  If it helps, you can to use it as a testimonial. It's the least I can do after everything your information has done for me.
										  Let me know if you'd like to see it and I'll send you a link to the file.
										  Best,
										  Your Name
										- If you're contacting them through social media, here is a template status update.
										  @NAME I made a video showing how I used PRODUCT NAME to get SPECIFIC RESULT. Would you like me to send it your way?
										- Then wait for a response. If you don't hear back within a week, try contacting them again through another means. It's most likely that they didn't see it.
										  collapsed:: true
											- When they reply. Send them the video. State again that they have your permission to use it as a case study or testimonial.
											- Also let them know that you'd like to help others benefit from the information in the same way. Offer to help with any other testimonial content that would be useful.
										- Thank her for her product + Let the creator know you have a review up and are letting people know about your experiences + Ask if there's anything you can do for her
									- Follow up with the influencer
									  collapsed:: true
										- Don't ask for something in return right away. Chances are the opportunity will come naturally. You've made a huge deposit in the reciprocity account.
										- Stay on their radar. Be helpful. Deliver value.
										  collapsed:: true
											- Do a few follow-up reviews, and repeat the last two steps. Leave your first review open-ended. Maybe there's more you can add onto it that you didn't cover in your first review. Maybe you're just giving a progress update. But follow up. Give more exposure.
											- Extremely effective method for networking + targetted traffic, especially useful to let beginners know if a product is good from an experts POV
						- Make several high-value blog comments
						- Retweet them + @ reply tweets of theirs
						- Join newsletter + Introduce self via newsletter reply
						- view every tweet reply to any fan/influencer via http://www.twimemachine.com http://www.allmytweets.net/connect.php http://www.twitario.com/en/ (3200 last tweets limit) - useful for seeing if a tweet I send will get a reply?
					- B) Demonstrate You're An Influencer
					  collapsed:: true
					  Promote Them on Own Platform
						- Include them in a piece of Money Magnet content
						  collapsed:: true
						  #MoneyMagnets https://workflowy.com/#/b36ff259663c
							- especially Interviews
							  #MoneyMagnets https://workflowy.com/#/82431268b439
						- Product Contest
						  collapsed:: true
							- Ask product creator for a free copy, otherwise buy a few copies. Hold a contest where people leave a comment as to how it would help them.
							- Check out the product here (affiliate link). If they buy it and they win then ill pay for their copy/refund money back; also try and get discount price for limited time.
					- C) Demonstrate You're A Friend
					  collapsed:: true
					  Solve Their Problems
						- Send Valuable Content
						  collapsed:: true
							- gems that they haven't seen e.g. research, news, websites, videos
							- "Hey I'm doing x, if you want details let me know and I'll be glad to send it through to you"
						- Fix Their Daily Problems
						  collapsed:: true
							- Find out their needs/problems and give it to them or connect them with someone
							- "What can I do right now to add value to what you're working on? How can I help?"
						- Offer My Unique Skills
						  collapsed:: true
							- If you truly have a knack for a topic, we’ll draw the line at top 5% in your industry/space, then you will find a way to add value to the guys in the top 1% or 0.5%. No doubt about it. You are going to find a way to expand his/her bottom line.
							- Ask if they want to know how they could improve their habits to improve their business/personal life
							- This complements my resources rather than sacrificing them
							- (Also they become a case study/testimonial/review for me)
							  collapsed:: true
								- free consultations e.g. time management, habit hacking coaching
								- Derek became known as conversion guy for 20 conversion vids in a month to promote and 2 closely related resources pages
							- Provide value to an internet marketing genius by improving his life habits
							  collapsed:: true
								- Derek Halpern
								  collapsed:: true
									- Benefit - Email offering a custom program with a choice of mastering any goal. Some of most popular/what I'm most effective as is help losing weight/gaining muscle/becoming healthy through diet or running
									- e.g. I can help you become healthy in just 15 minutes per week
									- I won't be there in-person but I will be the mobile coach in your phone telling you exactly what to do as you exercise
									- Reciprocal Benefit - free access to his course?; also use him as a case study; featured on his blog/podcast/youtube;
					- http://www.gll-getalife.com/get-paid/entry/how-to-get-noticed-by-established-guys-when-you-have-a-new-website
				- 2) Establish A Relationship
				  collapsed:: true
					- Ideal Prerequisites
					  collapsed:: true
						- A1) Done most of 1A - so high Reciprocity level
						- 1B) Established that I'm an Influencer
						- Have an introduction from another Influencer
						- Met bloggers with similar interests IRL e.g. conferences
						- This is a follow up - they've answered a question on twitter, a blog comment or webinar
					- Thank You Email
					  collapsed:: true
						- Very short and just say "hey, I really like your work.”
						- Ask an intriguing question to spark a discussion
						  collapsed:: true
							- Especially inquire about their story (self-interest) and they'll reciprocate
							- send a two- to three-paragraph e-mail which explains that I am familiar with their work, and ask one simple-to-answer but thought-provoking question in that e-mail related to their work or life philosophies. The goal is to start a dialogue so they take the time to answer future e-mails— not to ask for help. That can only come after at least three or four genuine e-mail exchanges.” An e-mail relationship was formed, and Komisar even e-mailed Marrinan a few days later with a link to a New York Times article on happiness.
					- Phone Interview (15-60m)
					  collapsed:: true
						- Show them benefit
						  collapsed:: true
							- e.g. credentials, audience size/type
							- product exposure "I'd like to interview you about your new book.."
						- Referral sometimes needed
						  collapsed:: true
							- if direct email/tweet doesn't work, get 2nd/3rd tier contacts to tell you how to approach them or to connect you both
						- Make it easy for them
						- Send them a list of pre-prepared questions ahead of time along with call in numbers and backup ways of connecting with them if something goes wrong with the call. If you're both using Gmail, send them a calendar invite so it gets on their schedule. Send them a confirmation message the day before the call.
					- Continue Connecting With Them
					  collapsed:: true
						- Be a Connector
						  collapsed:: true
							- Make introductions between them and others
							- "Hey, there's someone you need to meet who I think can really help you out with what you're doing."
							- Simple subject line.
							  collapsed:: true
								- I like using the form "Making an introduction: Mark, meet Charlie; Charlie, meet Mark" - it's simple, invokes names in a way that doesn't look automated, and the very phrasing of it foreshadows a connection.
							- A reason for the introduction.
							  collapsed:: true
								- Matching problem to need OR just helping 2 like-minded people meet each other
							- A short bio for each person (with relevance included)
							  collapsed:: true
								- Tell Mark what Charlie does (and who he does it for) and why Charlie is so remarkable. Add a link to Charlie's website and include his Twitter handle so Mark doesn't have to go hunting. Then give Mark the same treatment.
							- Invite them to connect
							  collapsed:: true
								- Here's where you can tell them the desired result, whether it's "you two should definitely get to know each other better" or "Mark, you might want to ask Charlie what he thinks of your upcoming project." Hint at the value that can be created by a meeting of the minds. And see what magic unfolds.
								- One big caution when doing this: Treat people's email addresses with respect. If you have access to a connection's private email address, do not use it in the To: or CC: field of the email, or you're sharing something private without permission. Use people's publicly available email addresses so you don't make an embarrassing mistake.
						- * Get their skype name and schedule chats with them
						- Continue giving value via emailing interesting stuff, replying to newsletters, blog comments, retweets, @replies
						- Mention and show them what I'm working on, writing etc
						- Emailing
						  collapsed:: true
							- recommending links
							- Ask for advice occasionally and later report back on how it goes

			- _[Learning Resources]_
				- Influencer strategy
					- What is your plan for reaching out to those two lists of bloggers? How can you connect with them in a genuine way and offer to help with projects each of them are working on? (make brief notes for each person listed in the above two steps)
					- Relationships always need to start from an honest, giving-without-expecting-anything place. How will you genuinely connect with the people you want to meet?
					- Make a list of the top 10 most popular or influential bloggers who write about similar or related topics to yours.
					- Creating strong relationships with other bloggers and entrepreneurs is an important key to getting the word out about your site. List some popular people you’d like to get to know here.
					- Make a list of 10 bloggers who write about similar or related topics but who started their blogs more recently (people who could become your peers in the next six months).

				- Deciding on commission %
					- Why performance-based pay
						- Accountability of the salesman
						- Motivation to work to best of ability
						- Higher profits for both salesman and owner
					- Offer Very High Commissions
					  #AutomatedPromotion https://workflowy.com/#/0930513d322c
					- Depends on margins and lifetime ROI
						- Digital products - easy to offer up to 80% commissions, possibly >100% if sales funnel is proven or its a recurring subscription
						- Restaurants - have tiny margins, 1-5%
				- Unique angle: Have affiliates send traffic to landing page and they get commissions for each affiliate click they do in your sales funnel
				  https://web.archive.org/web/20160418190554/https://cbpassiveincome30.com/livepage
					- you bring leads + pay $47 for signup page; he'll give commissions based on their CTR to affiliate links in his own email funnel
			- _Software_
				- Hosted
					- E-Junkie and Paypal Combination
						- E-Junkie is the easiest way on the planet to distribute a product and create an affiliate program. It only costs five bucks a month and integrates very well with paypal and both wishlist/amember.  This is the route I chose to take for Overcoming the Fear of Uncertainty.  E-Junkie allows people to easily sign up for the affiliate program, and it even allows me to create discount codes for the product, which can definitely help spur sales.
						- That said it certainly has it’s limitations.  You can not be paid on a recurring basis with this combination, so if you’re doing a monthly fee membership site, you’ll need to find another option.  It also gives affiliates very few options in terms of customization and exposure, so while it’s great starting out, it isn’t the best combination for a large scale project.
					- Clickbank
						- Clickbank is a little bit more expensive to get started, but it’s not too bad, just a $49.95 listing fee for your product.  They offer a variety of tools that E-junkie just doesn’t have.  You get listed in the Click Bank marketplace, so other people can find and promote your product.  They deal with all affiliate payouts and returns, which if your site grows will save you a LOT of time.  There are also options with Clickbank to setup recurring monthly payments.  So if you’re working on a pretty big site, this is probably the best starting point.
						- ClickBank is best for me when handling affiliates because it means I don’t have to worry about paying them or dealing with their taxes.
					- _Related: _Affiliate Programs
					  #AffiliatePrograms https://workflowy.com/#/db1d19acc662
				- Self Hosted
					- If you’ve already got a few products out there and have a large audience, then you might need something that gives you even more customization options than Clickbank.  Sure Clickbank can scale to about as big as you want to go, but if you’re really serious about building your affiliate program and distribution services, then you’ll want to consider something like 1Shoppingcart or Infusionsoft.
					- These bad boys don’t come cheap with 1SC starting at $99/month and Infusionsoft being a lot more than that.  So if you don’t already know what these are, there’s a good chance you should probably stick with E-Junkie or Clickbank.
					- JAM JROX
					- iDevAffiliate
					  http://www.idevdirect.com/
					- PostAffiliatePro
					  http://www.postaffiliatepro.com/

					- Media Buying
					  Buying adspace for a certain duration
						- You buy adspace or banner space on someone's web properties or web network. (This is not for beginners as well).

					- Solo Ads
						- Intro
							- AKA Links Blasts | Advertise my product/newsletter to another person's email list I think the easiest way to start generating money is to jump into SOLO ADS business. You just need two main things,one is email list and another thing is autoresponder. You sent email,people open, click and you making money. I will not explain what is solo ads and how it works, there are plenty of resources in internet about solo ads business,how they work and so on. So the first thing is EMAIL LIST. Don't think you could just scrap emails and use it for solo ads business. You need quality list, because you would sell the traffic for people and you MUST provide them only quality traffic. What is quality traffic? It's traffic which buys, which respons,which clicks and opens and which is in IM niche. How to get this type of email list? Simply, buy it. I will give facebook solo ads groups to join. After joining like 10 or more groups you need just post message and tell what you want to buy email list. In no time you will receive messages from people who are selling email list. You need to make sure he is solo ads seller and have testimonials. If he have good testimonials ask him to provide the screen shot of open rate ,click rate, this way you could be sure you will generate clicks and can calculate how much you can earn per day. For example if the person selling 10k email list and he is showing screen shot with 1% CTR,you could expect to generate about 100 clicks from sent email,so its easy minimum $30 per day. 1. Join facebook solo ads groups 2. Post message telling what you are buying email list. 3. Make sure person who contact you and offering list is solo ads seller and find his testimonials. 4. Ask for proof of open rate,click rate, top tier. 10k list would cost about $300. From 10k list you should receive minimum 1% click rate. open rate, click rate, bounce rate, spam rate. Make sure the person who contacted you and offering the list is solo ads seller and have testimonials, check if he/she is member in solo ads groups. If everything is right and you feel like he is trusted, you can make a deal, negotiate.

				- Choose the best ad campaign condition
					- [Audience-specific] - Find the most specific ad placement possible to find our ideal audience (e.g. a bodybuilding website, mens sexual health etc)
					- [Ad Campaign Duration] - Ideally 30 days
					- [Ad placement] Ideally above the folder i.e. the portion of the webpage which you can see as soon as it loads, without having to scroll down the page

			  collapsed:: true
				- CV Besure/CPV Labs SOPs
					- How to Setup a New Campaign on CPV Labs
						- Overview
							- **Description:** This SOP will tell you how to setup a new campaign on CPV Labs, our affiliate and advertiser tracking software.
							- **Benefit: **You will be able create and manage campaigns efficiently
							- Tell Budi that a new ad campaign has been created in the comments section of this subtask.
						- Login to CPV labs http://andro-plus.co/CPVtracking/login.php
						- Click New Campaign to create new one (choose one type of campaign, usually use 2 type of campaign, direct link or multiple option campaign
						- Direct Link & Landing page campaigns
						  Fill the Campaign Name
						  Choose CPV or CVC
						  Fill how much CPV or CVC is
						  Fill offer and URL, if only you use 1 offer, then write 100% on Share: %. if you use more than 1, then spit it
						  Save Campaign
						  Campaign URL is for landing page when you create campaign on ads web service
						- Multiple option campaigns
						  Fill the Campaign Name
						  Choose CPV or CVC
						  Fill how much CPV or CVC is
						  Fill LP and URL, then offer and URL
						  Save Campaign
						  Campaign URL is for landing page when you create campaign on ads web service
					- CV Besure - Review Campaign Stats
						- Calculate Ad Campaign ROI on spreadsheet (monthly)
							- Overview
								- This SOP allows you to check how well each ad campaign has performed. Complete this task the day before the deadline.
							- SOP
								- Go to Advertisers sheet ( MyCloud>Public>AP>AP Marketing )
									- See column 'Updated this sheet with ad campaign ROI?' and look for cells which have a red background and 'NO' in bold text
									- Check the column on it's left "Last date ad on site"
									- If today's date is before the 'Last date', then don't do anything and check the next cell
									- If today's date is after the 'Last date', then calculate begin to calculate the campaign ROI
									- See column '(Y/N) Updated this sheet with ad campaign ROI?'  N (No) if that row's ROI hasn't been updated yet and Y (Yes) if that row's ROI has been updated.
								- Calculating Ad Campaign ROI using CPV Labs:
									- Ask Budi for all data related to that ad campaign and input it into the spreadsheet
								- Calculating Ad Campaign ROI using Google Analytics:
									- Go to Referrals page https://www.google.com/analytics/web/?hl=en#report/trafficsources-referrals/
									- Change the custom date range to the start + end date of the ad campaign
									- Change the 'Conversions' column to 'Goal 1: Make a payment'
									- Multiply the number of 'Goal 1 Completions' by 100 and copy that number into the Advertisers sheet in the '$ total revenue' column
									- Divide '$ total revenue' by '$ total cost' and enter that number in the 'ROI' column
								- Evaluating ROI:
									- If ROI is above 1, then repurchase that ad campaign again immediately + update the 'Last date ad on site' cell
									- If ROI is below 1, then change the 'Updated this sheet with ad campaign ROI?' cell to blank with no colour
						- Review traffic/conversion stats (monthly)
							- Updating Google Analytics conversions
								- Go to https://www.google.com/analytics
									- Acquisition > All Traffic > Referrals
									- Then in the 'Conversions' column change 'eCommerce' to 'Goal 1: Make a Payment'
									- Click on the 'Make a payment (Goal 1 Completions)' column to make it sort by that variable
									- Scroll to the top of the page and adjust the date range to the past 30 days
									- Review the chart again: for each 'Goal 1 Completion', add '100' to $ total revenue + Lifetime sales revenue columns in the AP advertisers sheet
								- Calculating ROI:
									- If the campaign is now finished (check 'Late date that our ad will be on site') the calculate the ROI
									- Divide the '$ total revenue' by '$ total cost', then multiply this answer by 100 to get the ROI %. Add the ROI % to the sheet
					- Increasing sales via split-testing:
						- On a constant basis, you role will be to:
							- 1) Regularly add what you've learnt about conversion rate optimisation within this subtask
							  #ConversionRateOptimisation https://workflowy.com/#/0aba5a3c0de0
								- Use #HowToCreateASOP and #HowToUpdateASOP
							- 2) Every week create a new banner ad version (modify existing banners with new text/colours/font/graphics etc for split-testing)
							  NOTE: This is lower priority than other urgent changes. If the choice is being late on this task vs another, always finish the other task first.
							  
							  Upload the banners to the AP Banners folder (MyCloud>Public>AP>AP banners)
							  
							  Use https://app.asana.com/0/23195716314138/28357425542994 for all uploaded banners
							  
							  Conversion rate testing spreadsheet for AP (MyCloud>Public>AP>AP Marketing)
							- 3) Test and run experiments to optimise the conversion rate on our website
						- **Note:** Ad ID codes
						  3 letters for website e.g. Longecity = LCY
						  
						  Dimensions e.g. 728x90
						  
						  3 numbers for Ad ID e.g. 001 (Use a new ID if this is an entirely new ad. If you just modified an existing ad, use the same Ad ID)
						  
						  2 letters for Ad version e.g. AA for first version, AB for second etc. Do a new version anytime there is any dimensions, graphic or copy change from the original
						  
						  e.g. LCY-728x90-002AB

			- Keyword Optimisation
				- Keyword Research
				  id:: 644c0a78-0888-47c6-8f83-37c9d7866ee5
					- Principles
						- Choose a keyword which can be naturally inserted multiple times into the content + headline
						- On length
							- The shorter the keyword is, the harder it is to rank for (if all else is the same)
							- The shorter the keyword is, the higher is the search volume (if all else is the same)
					- Tools
						- Google Keyword Tool
						- Market Samurai
						  collapsed:: true
							- Write down a few ideal keywords in Market Samurai
								- Open Market Samurai
									- Click new keyword in sidebar
									- Add a few relevant keywords in 'Add Keywords' section
									- Generate Keywords
									- Keyword Analysis (bottom right)
								- Filters:
									- Have only these ones selected
									- SEO Traffic = min 300 (expected hits for #1 ranked site)
									- Title & Anchor Comp max 40k?
									- Title Competition max 40k?
									- URL Competition max 40k?
									- SEO Value min $0 (total value of traffic based on Adwords cost)
								- Analyse Keywords (middle right)
						- Long Tail Pro
							- Find Keywords > Seed Keywords > type in a few different keywords which are related to your topic
							- Customise Data and Pre-Filter
						- Moz
						- http://answerthepublic.com visual keyword search tool
					- Curating Keywords
						- Go play with your research tools for 15 minutes a day. Enter 20 different phrases and see which variants are the most popular. Or hunt down a phrase that doesn’t have much competition. It takes all of two seconds to look at a chart or graph, if you aren’t doing this you’re missing out.
						- Compile list of all potential keywords in niche
						- Compile niche keyword list to rank for: With SEMRush download all competitors keywords
						- http://www.copyblogger.com/keyword-research/
						- Use www.ubersuggest.org to search for your niche keyword
							- It'll give you the top keywords for it, which you can then google to see if there are influencers selling solutions to this audience

It’s worth spending money on because you can do keyword research on just about anything, not just your business idea. You’ll see what specific keywords people are using to search for something.
